7. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": "gcc/dwarf2out.h",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /* dwarf2out.h - Various declarations for functions found in dwarf2out.cc\\n2:    Copyright (C) 1998-2026 Free Software Foundation, Inc.\\n3: \\n4: This file is part of GCC.\\n5: \\n6: GCC is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under\\n7: the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free\\n8: Software Foundation; either version 3, or (at your option) any later\\n9: version.\\n10: \\n11: GCC is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY\\n12: WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or\\n13: FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License\\n14: for more details.\\n15: \\n16: You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\\n17: along with GCC; see the file COPYING3.  If not see\\n18: <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.  */\\n19: \\n20: #ifndef GCC_DWARF2OUT_H\\n21: #define GCC_DWARF2OUT_H 1\\n22: \\n23: #include \\\"dwarf2.h\\\"\\t/* ??? Remove this once only used by dwarf2foo.c.  */\\n24: \\n25: typedef struct die_struct *dw_die_ref;\\n26: typedef const struct die_struct *const_dw_die_ref;\\n27: \\n28: typedef struct dw_val_node *dw_val_ref;\\n29: typedef struct dw_cfi_node *dw_cfi_ref;\\n30: typedef struct dw_loc_descr_node *dw_loc_descr_ref;\\n31: typedef struct dw_loc_list_struct *dw_loc_list_ref;\\n32: typedef struct dw_discr_list_node *dw_discr_list_ref;\\n33: typedef struct dw_wide_int *dw_wide_int_ptr;\\n34: \\n35: \\n36: /* Call frames are described using a sequence of Call Frame\\n37:    Information instructions.  The register number, offset\\n38:    and address fields are provided as possible operands;\\n39:    their use is selected by the opcode field.  */\\n40: enum dw_cfi_oprnd_type: int {\\n41:   dw_cfi_oprnd_unused,\\n42:   dw_cfi_oprnd_reg_num,\\n43:   dw_cfi_oprnd_offset,\\n44:   dw_cfi_oprnd_addr,\\n45:   dw_cfi_oprnd_loc,\\n46:   dw_cfi_oprnd_cfa_loc\\n47: };\\n48: \\n49: typedef union GTY(()) {\\n50:   unsigned int GTY ((tag (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd_reg_num\\\"))) dw_cfi_reg_num;\\n51:   HOST_WIDE_INT GTY ((tag (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd_offset\\\"))) dw_cfi_offset;\\n52:   const char * GTY ((tag (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd_addr\\\"))) dw_cfi_addr;\\n53:   struct dw_loc_descr_node * GTY ((tag (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd_loc\\\"))) dw_cfi_loc;\\n54:   struct dw_cfa_location * GTY ((tag (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd_cfa_loc\\\")))\\n55:     dw_cfi_cfa_loc;\\n56: } dw_cfi_oprnd;\\n57: \\n58: struct GTY(()) dw_cfi_node {\\n59:   enum dwarf_call_frame_info dw_cfi_opc;\\n60:   dw_cfi_oprnd GTY ((desc (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd1_desc (%1.dw_cfi_opc)\\\")))\\n61:     dw_cfi_oprnd1;\\n62:   dw_cfi_oprnd GTY ((desc (\\\"dw_cfi_oprnd2_desc (%1.dw_cfi_opc)\\\")))\\n63:     dw_cfi_oprnd2;\\n64: };\\n65: \\n66: \\n67: typedef vec<dw_cfi_ref, va_gc> *cfi_vec;\\n68: \\n69: typedef struct dw_fde_node *dw_fde_ref;\\n70: \\n71: /* All call frame descriptions (FDE's) in the GCC generated DWARF\\n72:    refer to a single Common Information Entry (CIE), defined at\\n73:    the beginning of the .debug_frame section.  This use of a single\\n74:    CIE obviates the need to keep track of multiple CIE's\\n75:    in the DWARF generation routines below.  */\\n76: \\n77: struct GTY(()) dw_fde_node {\\n78:   tree decl;\\n79:   const char *dw_fde_begin;\\n80:   const char *dw_fde_current_label;\\n81:   const char *dw_fde_end;\\n82:   const char *dw_fde_vms_end_prologue;\\n83:   const char *dw_fde_vms_begin_epilogue;\\n84:   const char *dw_fde_second_begin;\\n85:   const char *dw_fde_second_end;\\n86:   cfi_vec dw_fde_cfi;\\n87:   int dw_fde_switch_cfi_index; /* Last CFI before switching sections.  */\\n88:   HOST_WIDE_INT stack_realignment;\\n89: \\n90:   unsigned funcdef_number;\\n91:   unsigned fde_index;\\n92: \\n93:   /* Dynamic realign argument pointer register.  */\\n94:   unsigned int drap_reg;\\n95:   /* Virtual dynamic realign argument pointer register.  */\\n96:   unsigned int vdrap_reg;\\n97:   /* These 3 flags are copied from rtl_data in function.h.  */\\n98:   unsigned all_throwers_are_sibcalls : 1;\\n99:   unsigned uses_eh_lsda : 1;\\n100:   unsigned nothrow : 1;\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.h\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /* dwarf2out.h - Various declarations for functions found in dwarf2out.cc\n2:    Copyright (C) 1998-2026 Free Software Foundation, Inc.\n3: \n4: This file is part of GCC.\n5: \n6: GCC is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under\n7: the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free\n8: Software Foundation; either version 3, or (at your option) any later\n9: version.\n10: \n11: GCC is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY\n12: WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or\n13: FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License\n14: for more details.\n15: \n16: You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n17: along with GCC; see the file COPYING3.  If not see\n18: <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.  */\n19: \n20: #ifndef GCC_DWARF2OUT_H\n21: #define GCC_DWARF2OUT_H 1\n22: \n23: #include \"dwarf2.h\"\t/* ??? Remove this once only used by dwarf2foo.c.  */\n24: \n25: typedef struct die_struct *dw_die_ref;\n26: typedef const struct die_struct *const_dw_die_ref;\n27: \n28: typedef struct dw_val_node *dw_val_ref;\n29: typedef struct dw_cfi_node *dw_cfi_ref;\n30: typedef struct dw_loc_descr_node *dw_loc_descr_ref;\n31: typedef struct dw_loc_list_struct *dw_loc_list_ref;\n32: typedef struct dw_discr_list_node *dw_discr_list_ref;\n33: typedef struct dw_wide_int *dw_wide_int_ptr;\n34: \n35: \n36: /* Call frames are described using a sequence of Call Frame\n37:    Information instructions.  The register number, offset\n38:    and address fields are provided as possible operands;\n39:    their use is selected by the opcode field.  */\n40: enum dw_cfi_oprnd_type: int {\n41:   dw_cfi_oprnd_unused,\n42:   dw_cfi_oprnd_reg_num,\n43:   dw_cfi_oprnd_offset,\n44:   dw_cfi_oprnd_addr,\n45:   dw_cfi_oprnd_loc,\n46:   dw_cfi_oprnd_cfa_loc\n47: };\n48: \n49: typedef union GTY(()) {\n50:   unsigned int GTY ((tag (\"dw_cfi_oprnd_reg_num\"))) dw_cfi_reg_num;\n51:   HOST_WIDE_INT GTY ((tag (\"dw_cfi_oprnd_offset\"))) dw_cfi_offset;\n52:   const char * GTY ((tag (\"dw_cfi_oprnd_addr\"))) dw_cfi_addr;\n53:   struct dw_loc_descr_node * GTY ((tag (\"dw_cfi_oprnd_loc\"))) dw_cfi_loc;\n54:   struct dw_cfa_location * GTY ((tag (\"dw_cfi_oprnd_cfa_loc\")))\n55:     dw_cfi_cfa_loc;\n56: } dw_cfi_oprnd;\n57: \n58: struct GTY(()) dw_cfi_node {\n59:   enum dwarf_call_frame_info dw_cfi_opc;\n60:   dw_cfi_oprnd GTY ((desc (\"dw_cfi_oprnd1_desc (%1.dw_cfi_opc)\")))\n61:     dw_cfi_oprnd1;\n62:   dw_cfi_oprnd GTY ((desc (\"dw_cfi_oprnd2_desc (%1.dw_cfi_opc)\")))\n63:     dw_cfi_oprnd2;\n64: };\n65: \n66: \n67: typedef vec<dw_cfi_ref, va_gc> *cfi_vec;\n68: \n69: typedef struct dw_fde_node *dw_fde_ref;\n70: \n71: /* All call frame descriptions (FDE's) in the GCC generated DWARF\n72:    refer to a single Common Information Entry (CIE), defined at\n73:    the beginning of the .debug_frame section.  This use of a single\n74:    CIE obviates the need to keep track of multiple CIE's\n75:    in the DWARF generation routines below.  */\n76: \n77: struct GTY(()) dw_fde_node {\n78:   tree decl;\n79:   const char *dw_fde_begin;\n80:   const char *dw_fde_current_label;\n81:   const char *dw_fde_end;\n82:   const char *dw_fde_vms_end_prologue;\n83:   const char *dw_fde_vms_begin_epilogue;\n84:   const char *dw_fde_second_begin;\n85:   const char *dw_fde_second_end;\n86:   cfi_vec dw_fde_cfi;\n87:   int dw_fde_switch_cfi_index; /* Last CFI before switching sections.  */\n88:   HOST_WIDE_INT stack_realignment;\n89: \n90:   unsigned funcdef_number;\n91:   unsigned fde_index;\n92: \n93:   /* Dynamic realign argument pointer register.  */\n94:   unsigned int drap_reg;\n95:   /* Virtual dynamic realign argument pointer register.  */\n96:   unsigned int vdrap_reg;\n97:   /* These 3 flags are copied from rtl_data in function.h.  */\n98:   unsigned all_throwers_are_sibcalls : 1;\n99:   unsigned uses_eh_lsda : 1;\n100:   unsigned nothrow : 1;\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.h"
  }
}
8. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": "gcc/debug.h",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /* Debug hooks for GCC.\\n2:    Copyright (C) 2001-2026 Free Software Foundation, Inc.\\n3: \\n4:    This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it\\n5:    under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the\\n6:    Free Software Foundation; either version 3, or (at your option) any\\n7:    later version.\\n8: \\n9:    This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\\n10:    but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\\n11:    MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\\n12:    GNU General Public License for more details.\\n13: \\n14:    You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\\n15:    along with this program; see the file COPYING3.  If not see\\n16:    <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.  */\\n17: \\n18: #ifndef GCC_DEBUG_H\\n19: #define GCC_DEBUG_H\\n20: \\n21: /* This structure contains hooks for the debug information output\\n22:    functions, accessed through the global instance debug_hooks set in\\n23:    toplev.cc according to command line options.  */\\n24: /* WARNING: Do not add new debug hook targets - DWARF will be the only\\n25:    way to speak debug to the middle-end once we are able to get rid of\\n26:    the remaining targets.  If you need alternate output formats instead\\n27:    generate them off the DWARF representation.  */\\n28: struct gcc_debug_hooks\\n29: {\\n30:   /* Initialize debug output.  MAIN_FILENAME is the name of the main\\n31:      input file.  */\\n32:   void (* init) (const char *main_filename);\\n33: \\n34:   /* Output debug symbols.  */\\n35:   void (* finish) (const char *main_filename);\\n36: \\n37:   /* Run cleanups necessary after early debug generation.  */\\n38:   void (* early_finish) (const char *main_filename);\\n39: \\n40:   /* Called from cgraph_optimize before starting to assemble\\n41:      functions/variables/toplevel asms.  */\\n42:   void (* assembly_start) (void);\\n43: \\n44:   /* Macro defined on line LINE with name and expansion TEXT.  */\\n45:   void (* define) (unsigned int line, const char *text);\\n46: \\n47:   /* MACRO undefined on line LINE.  */\\n48:   void (* undef) (unsigned int line, const char *macro);\\n49: \\n50:   /* Record the beginning of a new source file FILE from LINE number\\n51:      in the previous one.  */\\n52:   void (* start_source_file) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\\n53: \\n54:   /* Record the resumption of a source file.  LINE is the line number\\n55:      in the source file we are returning to.  */\\n56:   void (* end_source_file) (unsigned int line);\\n57: \\n58:   /* Record the beginning of block N, counting from 1 and not\\n59:      including the function-scope block, at LINE.  */\\n60:   void (* begin_block) (unsigned int line, unsigned int n, tree block);\\n61: \\n62:   /* Record the end of a block.  Arguments as for begin_block.  */\\n63:   void (* end_block) (unsigned int line, unsigned int n);\\n64: \\n65:   /* Returns nonzero if it is appropriate not to emit any debugging\\n66:      information for BLOCK, because it doesn't contain any\\n67:      instructions.  This may not be the case for blocks containing\\n68:      nested functions, since we may actually call such a function even\\n69:      though the BLOCK information is messed up.  Defaults to true.  */\\n70:   bool (* ignore_block) (const_tree);\\n71: \\n72:   /* Record a source file location at (FILE, LINE, COLUMN, DISCRIMINATOR).  */\\n73:   void (* source_line) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\\n74: \\t\\t\\tconst char *file, int discriminator, bool is_stmt);\\n75: \\n76:   /* Record a source file location for a DECL_IGNORED_P function.  */\\n77:   void (* set_ignored_loc) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\\n78: \\t\\t\\t    const char *file);\\n79: \\n80:   /* Called at start of prologue code.  LINE is the first line in the\\n81:      function.  */\\n82:   void (* begin_prologue) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\\n83: \\t\\t\\t   const char *file);\\n84: \\n85:   /* Called at end of prologue code.  LINE is the first line in the\\n86:      function.  */\\n87:   void (* end_prologue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\\n88: \\n89:   /* Called at beginning of epilogue code.  */\\n90:   void (* begin_epilogue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\\n91: \\n92:   /* Record end of epilogue code.  */\\n93:   void (* end_epilogue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\\n94: \\n95:   /* Called at start of function DECL, before it is declared.  */\\n96:   void (* begin_function) (tree decl);\\n97: \\n98:   /* Record end of function.  LINE is highest line number in function.  */\\n99:   void (* end_function) (unsigned int line);\\n100: \\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/debug.h\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /* Debug hooks for GCC.\n2:    Copyright (C) 2001-2026 Free Software Foundation, Inc.\n3: \n4:    This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it\n5:    under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the\n6:    Free Software Foundation; either version 3, or (at your option) any\n7:    later version.\n8: \n9:    This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n10:    but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n11:    MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n12:    GNU General Public License for more details.\n13: \n14:    You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n15:    along with this program; see the file COPYING3.  If not see\n16:    <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.  */\n17: \n18: #ifndef GCC_DEBUG_H\n19: #define GCC_DEBUG_H\n20: \n21: /* This structure contains hooks for the debug information output\n22:    functions, accessed through the global instance debug_hooks set in\n23:    toplev.cc according to command line options.  */\n24: /* WARNING: Do not add new debug hook targets - DWARF will be the only\n25:    way to speak debug to the middle-end once we are able to get rid of\n26:    the remaining targets.  If you need alternate output formats instead\n27:    generate them off the DWARF representation.  */\n28: struct gcc_debug_hooks\n29: {\n30:   /* Initialize debug output.  MAIN_FILENAME is the name of the main\n31:      input file.  */\n32:   void (* init) (const char *main_filename);\n33: \n34:   /* Output debug symbols.  */\n35:   void (* finish) (const char *main_filename);\n36: \n37:   /* Run cleanups necessary after early debug generation.  */\n38:   void (* early_finish) (const char *main_filename);\n39: \n40:   /* Called from cgraph_optimize before starting to assemble\n41:      functions/variables/toplevel asms.  */\n42:   void (* assembly_start) (void);\n43: \n44:   /* Macro defined on line LINE with name and expansion TEXT.  */\n45:   void (* define) (unsigned int line, const char *text);\n46: \n47:   /* MACRO undefined on line LINE.  */\n48:   void (* undef) (unsigned int line, const char *macro);\n49: \n50:   /* Record the beginning of a new source file FILE from LINE number\n51:      in the previous one.  */\n52:   void (* start_source_file) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\n53: \n54:   /* Record the resumption of a source file.  LINE is the line number\n55:      in the source file we are returning to.  */\n56:   void (* end_source_file) (unsigned int line);\n57: \n58:   /* Record the beginning of block N, counting from 1 and not\n59:      including the function-scope block, at LINE.  */\n60:   void (* begin_block) (unsigned int line, unsigned int n, tree block);\n61: \n62:   /* Record the end of a block.  Arguments as for begin_block.  */\n63:   void (* end_block) (unsigned int line, unsigned int n);\n64: \n65:   /* Returns nonzero if it is appropriate not to emit any debugging\n66:      information for BLOCK, because it doesn't contain any\n67:      instructions.  This may not be the case for blocks containing\n68:      nested functions, since we may actually call such a function even\n69:      though the BLOCK information is messed up.  Defaults to true.  */\n70:   bool (* ignore_block) (const_tree);\n71: \n72:   /* Record a source file location at (FILE, LINE, COLUMN, DISCRIMINATOR).  */\n73:   void (* source_line) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\n74: \t\t\tconst char *file, int discriminator, bool is_stmt);\n75: \n76:   /* Record a source file location for a DECL_IGNORED_P function.  */\n77:   void (* set_ignored_loc) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\n78: \t\t\t    const char *file);\n79: \n80:   /* Called at start of prologue code.  LINE is the first line in the\n81:      function.  */\n82:   void (* begin_prologue) (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\n83: \t\t\t   const char *file);\n84: \n85:   /* Called at end of prologue code.  LINE is the first line in the\n86:      function.  */\n87:   void (* end_prologue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\n88: \n89:   /* Called at beginning of epilogue code.  */\n90:   void (* begin_epilogue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\n91: \n92:   /* Record end of epilogue code.  */\n93:   void (* end_epilogue) (unsigned int line, const char *file);\n94: \n95:   /* Called at start of function DECL, before it is declared.  */\n96:   void (* begin_function) (tree decl);\n97: \n98:   /* Record end of function.  LINE is highest line number in function.  */\n99:   void (* end_function) (unsigned int line);\n100: \n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/debug.h"
  }
}
9. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 220,
  "path": "gcc/debug.h",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
  "startLine": 100
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"100: \\n101:   /* Register UNIT as the main translation unit.  Called from front-ends when\\n102:      they create their main translation unit.  */\\n103:   void (* register_main_translation_unit) (tree);\\n104: \\n105:   /* Debug information for a function DECL.  This might include the\\n106:      function name (a symbol), its parameters, and the block that\\n107:      makes up the function's body, and the local variables of the\\n108:      function.\\n109: \\n110:      This is only called for FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is part of the late\\n111:      debug pass and is called from rest_of_handle_final.\\n112: \\n113:      Location information is available at this point.\\n114: \\n115:      See the documentation for early_global_decl and late_global_decl\\n116:      for other entry points into the debugging back-ends for DECLs.  */\\n117:   void (* function_decl) (tree decl);\\n118: \\n119:   /* Debug information for a global DECL.  Called from the parser\\n120:      after the parsing process has finished.\\n121: \\n122:      This gets called for both variables and functions.\\n123: \\n124:      Location information is not available at this point, but it is a\\n125:      good probe point to get access to symbols before they get\\n126:      optimized away.\\n127: \\n128:      This hook may be called on VAR_DECLs or FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is up\\n129:      to the hook to use what it needs.  */\\n130:   void (* early_global_decl) (tree decl);\\n131: \\n132:   /* Augment debug information generated by early_global_decl with\\n133:      more complete debug info (if applicable).  Called from toplev.cc\\n134:      after the compilation proper has finished and cgraph information\\n135:      is available.\\n136: \\n137:      This gets called for both variables and functions.\\n138: \\n139:      Location information is usually available at this point, unless\\n140:      the hook is being called for a decl that has been optimized away.\\n141: \\n142:      This hook may be called on VAR_DECLs or FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is up\\n143:      to the hook to use what it needs.  */\\n144:   void (* late_global_decl) (tree decl);\\n145: \\n146:   /* Debug information for a type DECL.  Called from toplev.cc after\\n147:      compilation proper, also from various language front ends to\\n148:      record built-in types.  The second argument is properly a\\n149:      boolean, which indicates whether or not the type is a \\\"local\\\"\\n150:      type as determined by the language.  (It's not a boolean for\\n151:      legacy reasons.)  */\\n152:   void (* type_decl) (tree decl, int local);\\n153: \\n154:   /* Debug information for imported modules and declarations.  */\\n155:   void (* imported_module_or_decl) (tree decl, tree name,\\n156: \\t\\t\\t\\t    tree context, bool child,\\n157: \\t\\t\\t\\t    bool implicit);\\n158: \\n159:   /* Return true if a DIE for the tree is available and return a symbol\\n160:      and offset that can be used to refer to it externally.  */\\n161:   bool (* die_ref_for_decl) (tree, const char **, unsigned HOST_WIDE_INT *);\\n162: \\n163:   /* Early debug information for the tree is available at symbol plus\\n164:      offset externally.  */\\n165:   void (* register_external_die) (tree, const char *, unsigned HOST_WIDE_INT);\\n166: \\n167:   /* DECL is an inline function, whose body is present, but which is\\n168:      not being output at this point.  */\\n169:   void (* deferred_inline_function) (tree decl);\\n170: \\n171:   /* DECL is an inline function which is about to be emitted out of\\n172:      line.  The hook is useful to, e.g., emit abstract debug info for\\n173:      the inline before it gets mangled by optimization.  */\\n174:   void (* outlining_inline_function) (tree decl);\\n175: \\n176:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any CODE_LABEL insn whose\\n177:      LABEL_NAME is non-null.  */\\n178:   void (* label) (rtx_code_label *);\\n179: \\n180:   /* Called after the start and before the end of writing a PCH file.\\n181:      The parameter is 0 if after the start, 1 if before the end.  */\\n182:   void (* handle_pch) (unsigned int);\\n183: \\n184:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any NOTE_INSN_VAR_LOCATION note.  */\\n185:   void (* var_location) (rtx_insn *);\\n186: \\n187:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any NOTE_INSN_INLINE_ENTRY note.  */\\n188:   void (* inline_entry) (tree block);\\n189: \\n190:   /* Called from finalize_size_functions for size functions so that their body\\n191:      can be encoded in the debug info to describe the layout of variable-length\\n192:      structures.  */\\n193:   void (* size_function) (tree decl);\\n194: \\n195:   /* Called from final_scan_insn if there is a switch between hot and cold\\n196:      text sections.  */\\n197:   void (* switch_text_section) (void);\\n198: \\n199:   /* Called from grokdeclarator.  Replaces the anonymous name with the\\n200:      type name.  */\\n201:   void (* set_name) (tree, tree);\\n202: \\n203:   /* This is 1 if the debug writer wants to see start and end commands for the\\n204:      main source files, and 0 otherwise.  */\\n205:   int start_end_main_source_file;\\n206: \\n207:   /* The type of symtab field used by these debug hooks.  This is one\\n208:      of the TYPE_SYMTAB_IS_xxx values defined in tree.h.  */\\n209:   int tree_type_symtab_field;\\n210: };\\n211: \\n212: extern const struct gcc_debug_hooks *debug_hooks;\\n213: \\n214: /* The do-nothing hooks.  */\\n215: extern void debug_nothing_void (void);\\n216: extern void debug_nothing_charstar (const char *);\\n217: extern void debug_nothing_int_int_charstar (unsigned int, unsigned int,\\n218: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t    const char *);\\n219: extern void debug_nothing_int_charstar (unsigned int, const char *);\\n220: extern void debug_nothing_int_int_charstar_int_bool (unsigned int,\\n221: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/debug.h%3FL100-220\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "100: \n101:   /* Register UNIT as the main translation unit.  Called from front-ends when\n102:      they create their main translation unit.  */\n103:   void (* register_main_translation_unit) (tree);\n104: \n105:   /* Debug information for a function DECL.  This might include the\n106:      function name (a symbol), its parameters, and the block that\n107:      makes up the function's body, and the local variables of the\n108:      function.\n109: \n110:      This is only called for FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is part of the late\n111:      debug pass and is called from rest_of_handle_final.\n112: \n113:      Location information is available at this point.\n114: \n115:      See the documentation for early_global_decl and late_global_decl\n116:      for other entry points into the debugging back-ends for DECLs.  */\n117:   void (* function_decl) (tree decl);\n118: \n119:   /* Debug information for a global DECL.  Called from the parser\n120:      after the parsing process has finished.\n121: \n122:      This gets called for both variables and functions.\n123: \n124:      Location information is not available at this point, but it is a\n125:      good probe point to get access to symbols before they get\n126:      optimized away.\n127: \n128:      This hook may be called on VAR_DECLs or FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is up\n129:      to the hook to use what it needs.  */\n130:   void (* early_global_decl) (tree decl);\n131: \n132:   /* Augment debug information generated by early_global_decl with\n133:      more complete debug info (if applicable).  Called from toplev.cc\n134:      after the compilation proper has finished and cgraph information\n135:      is available.\n136: \n137:      This gets called for both variables and functions.\n138: \n139:      Location information is usually available at this point, unless\n140:      the hook is being called for a decl that has been optimized away.\n141: \n142:      This hook may be called on VAR_DECLs or FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is up\n143:      to the hook to use what it needs.  */\n144:   void (* late_global_decl) (tree decl);\n145: \n146:   /* Debug information for a type DECL.  Called from toplev.cc after\n147:      compilation proper, also from various language front ends to\n148:      record built-in types.  The second argument is properly a\n149:      boolean, which indicates whether or not the type is a \"local\"\n150:      type as determined by the language.  (It's not a boolean for\n151:      legacy reasons.)  */\n152:   void (* type_decl) (tree decl, int local);\n153: \n154:   /* Debug information for imported modules and declarations.  */\n155:   void (* imported_module_or_decl) (tree decl, tree name,\n156: \t\t\t\t    tree context, bool child,\n157: \t\t\t\t    bool implicit);\n158: \n159:   /* Return true if a DIE for the tree is available and return a symbol\n160:      and offset that can be used to refer to it externally.  */\n161:   bool (* die_ref_for_decl) (tree, const char **, unsigned HOST_WIDE_INT *);\n162: \n163:   /* Early debug information for the tree is available at symbol plus\n164:      offset externally.  */\n165:   void (* register_external_die) (tree, const char *, unsigned HOST_WIDE_INT);\n166: \n167:   /* DECL is an inline function, whose body is present, but which is\n168:      not being output at this point.  */\n169:   void (* deferred_inline_function) (tree decl);\n170: \n171:   /* DECL is an inline function which is about to be emitted out of\n172:      line.  The hook is useful to, e.g., emit abstract debug info for\n173:      the inline before it gets mangled by optimization.  */\n174:   void (* outlining_inline_function) (tree decl);\n175: \n176:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any CODE_LABEL insn whose\n177:      LABEL_NAME is non-null.  */\n178:   void (* label) (rtx_code_label *);\n179: \n180:   /* Called after the start and before the end of writing a PCH file.\n181:      The parameter is 0 if after the start, 1 if before the end.  */\n182:   void (* handle_pch) (unsigned int);\n183: \n184:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any NOTE_INSN_VAR_LOCATION note.  */\n185:   void (* var_location) (rtx_insn *);\n186: \n187:   /* Called from final_scan_insn for any NOTE_INSN_INLINE_ENTRY note.  */\n188:   void (* inline_entry) (tree block);\n189: \n190:   /* Called from finalize_size_functions for size functions so that their body\n191:      can be encoded in the debug info to describe the layout of variable-length\n192:      structures.  */\n193:   void (* size_function) (tree decl);\n194: \n195:   /* Called from final_scan_insn if there is a switch between hot and cold\n196:      text sections.  */\n197:   void (* switch_text_section) (void);\n198: \n199:   /* Called from grokdeclarator.  Replaces the anonymous name with the\n200:      type name.  */\n201:   void (* set_name) (tree, tree);\n202: \n203:   /* This is 1 if the debug writer wants to see start and end commands for the\n204:      main source files, and 0 otherwise.  */\n205:   int start_end_main_source_file;\n206: \n207:   /* The type of symtab field used by these debug hooks.  This is one\n208:      of the TYPE_SYMTAB_IS_xxx values defined in tree.h.  */\n209:   int tree_type_symtab_field;\n210: };\n211: \n212: extern const struct gcc_debug_hooks *debug_hooks;\n213: \n214: /* The do-nothing hooks.  */\n215: extern void debug_nothing_void (void);\n216: extern void debug_nothing_charstar (const char *);\n217: extern void debug_nothing_int_int_charstar (unsigned int, unsigned int,\n218: \t\t\t\t\t    const char *);\n219: extern void debug_nothing_int_charstar (unsigned int, const char *);\n220: extern void debug_nothing_int_int_charstar_int_bool (unsigned int,\n221: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/debug.h%3FL100-220"
  }
}

This\\n665: \",\"endLine\":665,\"startLine\":662},{\"content\":\"206:    --  d.2  Allow statements in declarative part\\n207:    --  d.3  Output debugging information from Exp_Unst\\n208:    --  d.4  Do not delete generated C file in case of errors\\n209: \",\"endLine\":209,\"startLine\":206},{\"content\":\"60: \\n61:    --  dA   All entities included in representation information output\\n62:    --  dB   Output debug encoding of type names and variants\\n63:    --  dC   Output debugging information on check suppression\\n64:    --  dD   Delete elaboration checks in inner level routines\\n65: \",\"endLine\":65,\"startLine\":60}],\"file\":\"gcc/ada/debug.adb\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/debug.adb\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"7045: \\n7046: \\t* exp_dbug.adb: Use Sem_Util.Set_Debug_Info_Needed (not\\n7047: \\tEinfo.Set_Needs_Debug_Info)\\n7048: \\n7049: \",\"endLine\":7049,\"startLine\":7045},{\"content\":\"2846: \\tattribute, parallel descriptive type attached to another\\n2847: \\ttype for debug info generation purposes.\\n2848: \\t* utils.c (add_parallel_type): New function, register\\n2849: \",\"endLine\":2849,\"startLine\":2846},{\"content\":\"2523: \\t(Expand_N_Protected_Body): Code reformatting. Replace the wrapper body\\n2524: \\tcreation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Bodies.\\n2525: \\t(Expand_N_Protected_Type_Declaration): Code reformatting. Replace the\\n2526: \\twrapper spec creation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Specs.\\n2527: \\t(Expand_N_Task_Body): Replace the wrapper body creation\\n2528: \\tmechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Bodies.\\n2529: \\t(Expand_N_Task_Type_Declaration): Replace the wrapper spec\\n2530: \\tcreation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Specs.\\n2531: \\t(Is_Private_Primitive_Subprogram): New subprogram.\\n2532: \",\"endLine\":2532,\"startLine\":2523}],\"file\":\"gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2008\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2008\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"44:    These functions will be used by the CTF container to give access to its\\n45:    consumers (CTF/BTF) to various debug information available in DWARF DIE.\\n46:    Direct access to debug information in GCC dwarf structures by the consumers\\n47:    of CTF/BTF information is not ideal.  */\\n48: \\n49: /* Source location information.  */\\n50: \\n51: extern const char * ctf_get_die_loc_file (dw_die_ref);\\n52: extern unsigned int ctf_get_die_loc_line (dw_die_ref);\\n53: extern unsigned int ctf_get_die_loc_col (dw_die_ref);\\n54: \\n55: \",\"endLine\":55,\"startLine\":44},{\"content\":\"28: \\n29: /* Debug Format Interface.  Used in dwarf2out.cc.  */\\n30: \\n31: extern void ctf_debug_init (void);\\n32: extern void ctf_debug_init_postprocess (bool);\\n33: extern bool ctf_do_die (dw_die_ref);\\n34: extern void ctf_debug_early_finish (const char *);\\n35: extern void ctf_debug_finish (void);\\n36: \\n37: /* Wrappers for CTF/BTF to fetch information from GCC DWARF DIE.  Used in\\n38:    ctfc.cc.\\n39: \",\"endLine\":39,\"startLine\":28},{\"content\":\"39: \\n40:    A CTF container does not store all debug information internally.  Some of\\n41:    the info is fetched indirectly via the DIE reference available in each CTF\\n42:    container entry.\\n43: \\n44: \",\"endLine\":44,\"startLine\":39}],\"file\":\"gcc/dwarf2ctf.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2ctf.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"171: ctf_dvdef_ref\\n172: ctf_dvd_lookup (const ctf_container_ref ctfc, dw_die_ref die)\\n173: {\\n174:   ctf_dvdef_t entry;\\n175:   entry.dvd_key = die;\\n176: \\n177:   ctf_dvdef_ref * slot = ctfc->ctfc_vars->find_slot (&entry, NO_INSERT);\\n178: \\n179: \",\"endLine\":179,\"startLine\":171},{\"content\":\"205: bool\\n206: ctf_dvd_ignore_lookup (const ctf_container_ref ctfc, dw_die_ref die)\\n207: {\\n208:   ctf_dvdef_t entry;\\n209:   entry.dvd_key = die;\\n210: \\n211:   ctf_dvdef_ref * slot = ctfc->ctfc_ignore_vars->find_slot (&entry, NO_INSERT);\\n212: \\n213: \",\"endLine\":213,\"startLine\":205},{\"content\":\"836: ctf_add_function (ctf_container_ref ctfc, uint32_t flag, const char * name,\\n837: \\t\\t  const ctf_funcinfo_t * ctc, dw_die_ref die,\\n838: \\t\\t  bool from_global_func, int linkage)\\n839: \",\"endLine\":839,\"startLine\":836}],\"file\":\"gcc/ctfc.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ctfc.cc\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"17: \\n18: #ifndef GCC_DEBUG_H\\n19: #define GCC_DEBUG_H\\n20: \\n21: /* This structure contains hooks for the debug information output\\n22:    functions, accessed through the global instance debug_hooks set in\\n23:    toplev.cc according to command line options.  */\\n24: /* WARNING: Do not add new debug hook targets - DWARF will be the only\\n25:    way to speak debug to the middle-end once we are able to get rid of\\n26:    the remaining targets.  If you need alternate output formats instead\\n27: \",\"endLine\":27,\"startLine\":17},{\"content\":\"153: \\n154:   /* Debug information for imported modules and declarations.  */\\n155:   void (* imported_module_or_decl) (tree decl, tree name,\\n156: \",\"endLine\":156,\"startLine\":153},{\"content\":\"118: \\n119:   /* Debug information for a global DECL.  Called from the parser\\n120:      after the parsing process has finished.\\n121: \",\"endLine\":121,\"startLine\":118}],\"file\":\"gcc/debug.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/debug.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2505: void\\n2506: debuginfo_init (void)\\n2507: {\\n2508: \",\"endLine\":2508,\"startLine\":2505},{\"content\":\"2537: void\\n2538: debuginfo_stop (void)\\n2539: {\\n2540: \",\"endLine\":2540,\"startLine\":2537},{\"content\":\"2518: void\\n2519: debuginfo_fini (void)\\n2520: {\\n2521:   if (debuginfo_dump_file)\\n2522:     dump_end (debuginfo_dump_nr, debuginfo_dump_file);\\n2523:   if (debuginfo_early_dump_file)\\n2524:     dump_end (debuginfo_early_dump_nr, debuginfo_early_dump_file);\\n2525: }\\n2526: \",\"endLine\":2526,\"startLine\":2518}],\"file\":\"gcc/cgraphunit.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/cgraphunit.cc\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"147: \\n148:   pd->add_region_creation_events (reg, capacity, loc_info, *this);\\n149: \\n150:   if (debug)\\n151:     add_event (std::make_unique<region_creation_event_debug> (reg, capacity,\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t      loc_info));\\n153: }\\n154: \",\"endLine\":154,\"startLine\":147},{\"content\":\"114: \\n115: DEBUG_FUNCTION void\\n116: checker_path::debug () const\\n117: {\\n118: \",\"endLine\":118,\"startLine\":114},{\"content\":\"136: void\\n137: checker_path::add_region_creation_events (pending_diagnostic *pd,\\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t  const region *reg,\\n139: \",\"endLine\":139,\"startLine\":136}],\"file\":\"gcc/analyzer/checker-path.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/analyzer/checker-path.cc\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "432: \n433: extern void debug_dwarf (void);\n434: struct die_struct;\n435: extern void debug_dwarf_die (struct die_struct *);\n436: extern void debug_dwarf_loc_descr (dw_loc_descr_ref);\n437: extern void debug (die_struct &ref);\n438: extern void debug (die_struct *ptr);\n439: extern void dwarf2out_set_demangle_name_func (const char *(*) (const char *));\n440: #ifdef VMS_DEBUGGING_INFO\n441: extern void dwarf2out_vms_debug_main_pointer (void);\n442: #endif\n443: ",
            "endLine": 443,
            "startLine": 432
          },
          {
            "content": "527: extern unsigned HOST_WIDE_INT AT_unsigned (dw_attr_node *);\n528: extern unsigned get_AT_unsigned (dw_die_ref, enum dwarf_attribute);\n529: extern int get_AT_flag (dw_die_ref, enum dwarf_attribute);\n530: \n531: extern void add_name_attribute (dw_die_ref, const char *);\n532: \n533: extern dw_die_ref new_die_raw (enum dwarf_tag);\n534: extern dw_die_ref base_type_die (tree, bool);\n535: \n536: extern dw_die_ref lookup_decl_die (tree);\n537: extern dw_die_ref lookup_type_die (tree);\n538: \n539: extern dw_die_ref dw_get_die_child (dw_die_ref);\n540: extern dw_die_ref dw_get_die_sib (dw_die_ref);\n541: extern dw_die_ref dw_get_die_parent (dw_die_ref);\n542: extern enum dwarf_tag dw_get_die_tag (dw_die_ref);\n543: \n544: ",
            "endLine": 544,
            "startLine": 527
          },
          {
            "content": "71: /* All call frame descriptions (FDE's) in the GCC generated DWARF\n72:    refer to a single Common Information Entry (CIE), defined at\n73:    the beginning of the .debug_frame section.  This use of a single\n74:    CIE obviates the need to keep track of multiple CIE's\n75: ",
            "endLine": 75,
            "startLine": 71
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/dwarf2out.h",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.h"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "950: {\n951:   if (!btf_debuginfo_p () || !die || !ref_dvd)\n952:     return;\n953: ",
            "endLine": 953,

          {
            "content": "924: {\n925:   if (!btf_debuginfo_p () || !die || !ref_dtd)\n926:     return;\n927: ",
            "endLine": 927,
            "startLine": 924

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "328: \n329: /* Concrete subclass of region_creation_event.\n330:    Generates a debug message intended for analyzer developers.  */\n331: \n332: class region_creation_event_debug : public region_creation_event\n333: {\n334: ",
            "endLine": 334,
            "startLine": 328
          },
          {
            "content": "334: public:\n335:   region_creation_event_debug (const region *reg, tree capacity,\n336: \t\t\t       const event_loc_info &loc_info)\n337:   : region_creation_event (loc_info),\n338:     m_reg (reg), m_capacity (capacity)\n339: ",
            "endLine": 339,
            "startLine": 334
          },
          {
            "content": "175: \n176:   debug_event (const event_loc_info &loc_info,\n177: \t       const char *desc)\n178:   : checker_event (event_kind::debug, loc_info),\n179:     m_desc (xstrdup (desc))\n180: ",
            "endLine": 180,
            "startLine": 175
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/analyzer/checker-event.h",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {

          },
          {

          {
            "content": "303: \n304: /* class region_creation_event : public checker_event.  */\n305: \n306: region_creation_event::region_creation_event (const event_loc_info &loc_info)\n307: : checker_event (event_kind::region_creation, loc_info)\n308: {\n309: ",
            "endLine": 309,
            "startLine": 303
          },
          {
            "content": "456: \t    {\n457: \t      /* Append debugging information about this event.  */\n458: \n459: ",
            "endLine": 459,
            "startLine": 456
          },
          {
            "content": "368: void\n369: region_creation_event_debug::print_desc (pretty_printer &pp) const\n370: {\n371:   pp_string (&pp, \"region creation: \");\n372:   m_reg->dump_to_pp (&pp, true);\n373: ",

        "file": "gcc/analyzer/checker-event.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/analyzer/checker-event.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "593: \n594: \t* einfo.ads, einfo.adb: Flag 232 is now Implemented_By_Entry.\n595: \t(Implemented_By_Entry, Set_Implemented_By_Entry): New routines.\n596: \t(Write_Entry_Flags): Add an entry for Implemented_By_Entry.\n597: \t(Renamed_In_Spec): New flag\n598: ",
            "endLine": 598,
            "startLine": 593
          },
          {
            "content": "2975: \n2976: \t* einfo.ads, einfo.adb (Debug_Renaming_Link): Change to return Node25.\n2977: \t(Set_Debug_Renaming_Link): Change to set Node25.\n2978: \t(Write_Field13_Name): Remove case for E_Enumeration_Literal.\n2979: ",
            "endLine": 2979,
            "startLine": 2975
          },
          {
            "content": "4933: \t<E_Record_Type>: Do not explicitly defer finalizing the type.\n4934: \tAdjust for write_record_type_debug_info renaming.\n4935: \t<E_Subprogram_Type>: Likewise.\n4936: ",
            "endLine": 4936,
            "startLine": 4933
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2007",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2007"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1996: \t(call_clobbered_vars): Change to bitmap.\n1997: \t(dump_points_to_info): Declare.\n1998: \t(debug_points_to_info): Declare.\n1999: \t(walk_use_def_chains_fn): New type.\n2000: ",
            "endLine": 2000,
            "startLine": 1996
          },
          {
            "content": "13249: \t(find_alias_tag): Rename from find_alias_leader.\n13250: \t(dump_alias_info): New function.\n13251: \t(debug_alias_info): New function.\n13252: \t(htab_hash_var): New function.\n13253: ",
            "endLine": 13253,
            "startLine": 13249
          },
          {
            "content": "1914: \t(coalesce_ssa_name): Create coalesce list if requested.  Add more\n1915: \tdebug output.\n1916: \t(assign_vars): Add additional debug info.\n1917: \t(remove_ssa_form): Perform TER after assign_vars().\n1918: ",
            "endLine": 1918,
            "startLine": 1914
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ChangeLog.tree-ssa",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ChangeLog.tree-ssa"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "662: \n663:    --  dV   Enable viewing of all symbols in debugger. Causes debug information\n664:    --       to be generated for all symbols, including internal symbols. This\n665: ",
            "endLine": 665,
            "startLine": 662
          },
          {
            "content": "206:    --  d.2  Allow statements in declarative part\n207:    --  d.3  Output debugging information from Exp_Unst\n208:    --  d.4  Do not delete generated C file in case of errors\n209: ",
            "endLine": 209,
            "startLine": 206
          },
          {
            "content": "60: \n61:    --  dA   All entities included in representation information output\n62:    --  dB   Output debug encoding of type names and variants\n63:    --  dC   Output debugging information on check suppression\n64:    --  dD   Delete elaboration checks in inner level routines\n65: ",
            "endLine": 65,
            "startLine": 60
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ada/debug.adb",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/debug.adb"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "7045: \n7046: \t* exp_dbug.adb: Use Sem_Util.Set_Debug_Info_Needed (not\n7047: \tEinfo.Set_Needs_Debug_Info)\n7048: \n7049: ",
            "endLine": 7049,
            "startLine": 7045
          },
          {
            "content": "2846: \tattribute, parallel descriptive type attached to another\n2847: \ttype for debug info generation purposes.\n2848: \t* utils.c (add_parallel_type): New function, register\n2849: ",
            "endLine": 2849,
            "startLine": 2846
          },
          {
            "content": "2523: \t(Expand_N_Protected_Body): Code reformatting. Replace the wrapper body\n2524: \tcreation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Bodies.\n2525: \t(Expand_N_Protected_Type_Declaration): Code reformatting. Replace the\n2526: \twrapper spec creation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Specs.\n2527: \t(Expand_N_Task_Body): Replace the wrapper body creation\n2528: \tmechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Bodies.\n2529: \t(Expand_N_Task_Type_Declaration): Replace the wrapper spec\n2530: \tcreation mechanism with a call to Build_Wrapper_Specs.\n2531: \t(Is_Private_Primitive_Subprogram): New subprogram.\n2532: ",
            "endLine": 2532,
            "startLine": 2523
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2008",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/ChangeLog-2008"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "44:    These functions will be used by the CTF container to give access to its\n45:    consumers (CTF/BTF) to various debug information available in DWARF DIE.\n46:    Direct access to debug information in GCC dwarf structures by the consumers\n47:    of CTF/BTF information is not ideal.  */\n48: \n49: /* Source location information.  */\n50: \n51: extern const char * ctf_get_die_loc_file (dw_die_ref);\n52: extern unsigned int ctf_get_die_loc_line (dw_die_ref);\n53: extern unsigned int ctf_get_die_loc_col (dw_die_ref);\n54: \n55: ",
            "endLine": 55,
            "startLine": 44
          },
          {
            "content": "28: \n29: /* Debug Format Interface.  Used in dwarf2out.cc.  */\n30: \n31: extern void ctf_debug_init (void);\n32: extern void ctf_debug_init_postprocess (bool);\n33: extern bool ctf_do_die (dw_die_ref);\n34: extern void ctf_debug_early_finish (const char *);\n35: extern void ctf_debug_finish (void);\n36: \n37: /* Wrappers for CTF/BTF to fetch information from GCC DWARF DIE.  Used in\n38:    ctfc.cc.\n39: ",
            "endLine": 39,
            "startLine": 28
          },
          {
            "content": "39: \n40:    A CTF container does not store all debug information internally.  Some of\n41:    the info is fetched indirectly via the DIE reference available in each CTF\n42:    container entry.\n43: \n44: ",
            "endLine": 44,
            "startLine": 39
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/dwarf2ctf.h",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2ctf.h"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "171: ctf_dvdef_ref\n172: ctf_dvd_lookup (const ctf_container_ref ctfc, dw_die_ref die)\n173: {\n174:   ctf_dvdef_t entry;\n175:   entry.dvd_key = die;\n176: \n177:   ctf_dvdef_ref * slot = ctfc->ctfc_vars->find_slot (&entry, NO_INSERT);\n178: \n179: ",
            "endLine": 179,
            "startLine": 171
          },
          {
            "content": "205: bool\n206: ctf_dvd_ignore_lookup (const ctf_container_ref ctfc, dw_die_ref die)\n207: {\n208:   ctf_dvdef_t entry;\n209:   entry.dvd_key = die;\n210: \n211:   ctf_dvdef_ref * slot = ctfc->ctfc_ignore_vars->find_slot (&entry, NO_INSERT);\n212: \n213: ",
            "endLine": 213,
            "startLine": 205
          },
          {
            "content": "836: ctf_add_function (ctf_container_ref ctfc, uint32_t flag, const char * name,\n837: \t\t  const ctf_funcinfo_t * ctc, dw_die_ref die,\n838: \t\t  bool from_global_func, int linkage)\n839: ",
            "endLine": 839,
            "startLine": 836
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ctfc.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ctfc.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "17: \n18: #ifndef GCC_DEBUG_H\n19: #define GCC_DEBUG_H\n20: \n21: /* This structure contains hooks for the debug information output\n22:    functions, accessed through the global instance debug_hooks set in\n23:    toplev.cc according to command line options.  */\n24: /* WARNING: Do not add new debug hook targets - DWARF will be the only\n25:    way to speak debug to the middle-end once we are able to get rid of\n26:    the remaining targets.  If you need alternate output formats instead\n27: ",
            "endLine": 27,
            "startLine": 17
          },
          {
            "content": "153: \n154:   /* Debug information for imported modules and declarations.  */\n155:   void (* imported_module_or_decl) (tree decl, tree name,\n156: ",
            "endLine": 156,
            "startLine": 153
          },
          {
            "content": "118: \n119:   /* Debug information for a global DECL.  Called from the parser\n120:      after the parsing process has finished.\n121: ",
            "endLine": 121,
            "startLine": 118
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/debug.h",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/debug.h"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2505: void\n2506: debuginfo_init (void)\n2507: {\n2508: ",

            "startLine": 2505
          },
          {
            "content": "2537: void\n2538: debuginfo_stop (void)\n2539: {\n2540: ",
            "endLine": 2540,
            "startLine": 2537
          },
          {
            "content": "2518: void\n2519: debuginfo_fini (void)\n2520: {\n2521:   if (debuginfo_dump_file)\n2522:     dump_end (debuginfo_dump_nr, debuginfo_dump_file);\n2523:   if (debuginfo_early_dump_file)\n2524:     dump_end (debuginfo_early_dump_nr, debuginfo_early_dump_file);\n2525: }\n2526: ",
            "endLine": 2526,
            "startLine": 2518
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/cgraphunit.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",

        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/cgraphunit.cc"
      },
      {

20. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Search for DWARF DIE creation and debug hook implementations",
  "prompt": "In the gcc repository (github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333), find:\n1. The definition of `dwarf2_debug_hooks` in dwarf2out.cc\n2. Key functions that create DIEs (Debug Info Entries), particularly `new_die_raw` and `new_die`\n3. Functions that output DWARF information like `output_die`, `output_attribute`, `output_line_info`\n4. Line number table emission functions\n\nProvide file paths and line numbers for these key locations.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ac732ce695c7467dc",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Based on my extensive searches, I have found the key DWARF2 functions. Let me compile the complete findings now:\n\n## Summary\n\nI have successfully located the key DWARF2 debugging functions in the gcc repository (github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333) in the file **gcc/dwarf2out.cc**. Here are the findings:\n\n### Key Function Definitions:\n\n1. **`new_die_raw`** - Line **5850**\n   - Allocates a new DIE (Debug Info Entry) structure with a specified tag\n   - Returns: `dw_die_ref`\n   - File: `/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`\n\n2. **`new_die`** - Line **5863**\n   - Creates and returns a new DIE with a parent\n   - Handles limbo DIE management for parentless DIEs\n   - File: `/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`\n\n3. **`output_die`** - Line **10830**\n   - Outputs the DIE and its attributes recursively\n   - Calls itself recursively to generate definitions of child DIEs\n   - File: `/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`\n\n4. **`output_one_line_info_table`** - Line **12982**\n   - Outputs one line number table into the .debug_line section\n   - Handles line number state machine encoding\n   - File: `/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`\n\n5. **`output_line_info`** - Line **13137**\n   - Main function for outputting source line number correspondence information\n   - Outputs to .debug_line section\n   - Takes a `prologue_only` parameter\n   - File: `/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`\n\n### Key Forward Declarations:\n\n- **`new_die`** forward declaration - Line **3791**\n- **`output_die`** forward declaration - Line **3849**\n- **`new_die_raw`** declaration in header - gcc/dwarf2out.h, Line **533**\n\n### DIE Creation and Manipulation Functions:\n\n- **`add_child_die`** - Line **5771** - Adds a DIE as the last child of another DIE\n- **`add_dwarf_attr`** - Referenced throughout for adding attributes to DIEs\n- **`output_attr_index_or_value`** and related attribute output functions\n\n### Helper Functions for Line Number Emission:\n\n- Helper functions referenced in **`output_one_line_info_table`** (lines 12982-13129) handle:\n  - Address setting (LI_set_address)\n  - Line setting (LI_set_line)\n  - File setting (LI_set_file)\n  - Column setting (LI_set_column)\n  - Discriminator setting (LI_set_discriminator)\n\nAll these key functions are implemented in **`/workspace/gcc/dwarf2out.cc`** and work together to generate DWARF2 debugging information including DIE creation, attribute assignment, and debug line information emission.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

If this points to the same function as builtin_function, the\\n605:      backend must add all of the builtins at program initialization time.  */\\n606:   tree (*builtin_function_ext_scope) (tree decl);\\n607: \\n608: \",\"endLine\":608,\"startLine\":596},{\"content\":\"589: \\n590:   /* Determine if a tree is a function parameter pack.  */\\n591:   bool (*function_parameter_pack_p) (const_tree);\\n592: \\n593: \",\"endLine\":593,\"startLine\":589}],\"file\":\"gcc/langhooks.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/langhooks.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"99: extern void codeview_end_block (unsigned int, unsigned int);\\n100: extern void codeview_abstract_function (tree);\\n101: \\n102: \",\"endLine\":102,\"startLine\":99},{\"content\":\"89: \\n90: /* Debug Format Interface. 45. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": "gcc/dwarf2out.cc",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: /* Output Dwarf2 format symbol table information from GCC.\\n2:    Copyright (C) 1992-2026 Free Software Foundation, Inc.\\n3:    Contributed by Gary Funck (gary@intrepid.com).\\n4:    Derived from DWARF 1 implementation of Ron Guilmette (rfg@monkeys.com).\\n5:    Extensively modified by Jason Merrill (jason@cygnus.com).\\n6: \\n7: This file is part of GCC.\\n8: \\n9: GCC is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under\\n10: the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free\\n11: Software Foundation; either version 3, or (at your option) any later\\n12: version.\\n13: \\n14: GCC is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY\\n15: WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or\\n16: FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU General Public License\\n17: for more details.\\n18: \\n19: You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\\n20: along with GCC; see the file COPYING3.  If not see\\n21: <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.  */\\n22: \\n23: /* TODO: Emit .debug_line header even when there are no functions, since\\n24: \\t   the file numbers are used by .debug_info.  Alternately, leave\\n25: \\t   out locations for types and decls.\\n26: \\t Avoid talking about ctors and op= for PODs.\\n27: \\t Factor out common prologue sequences into multiple CIEs.  */\\n28: \\n29: /* The first part of this file deals with the DWARF 2 frame unwind\\n30:    information, which is also used by the GCC efficient exception handling\\n31:    mechanism.  The second part, controlled only by an #ifdef\\n32:    DWARF2_DEBUGGING_INFO, deals with the other DWARF 2 debugging\\n33:    information.  */\\n34: \\n35: /* DWARF2 Abbreviation Glossary:\\n36: \\n37:    CFA = Canonical Frame Address\\n38: \\t   a fixed address on the stack which identifies a call frame.\\n39: \\t   We define it to be the value of SP just before the call insn.\\n40: \\t   The CFA register and offset, which may change during the course\\n41: \\t   of the function, are used to calculate its value at runtime.\\n42: \\n43:    CFI = Call Frame Instruction\\n44: \\t   an instruction for the DWARF2 abstract machine\\n45: \\n46:    CIE = Common Information Entry\\n47: \\t   information describing information common to one or more FDEs\\n48: \\n49:    DIE = Debugging Information Entry\\n50: \\n51:    FDE = Frame Description Entry\\n52: \\t   information describing the stack call frame, in particular,\\n53: \\t   how to restore registers\\n54: \\n55:    DW_CFA_... = DWARF2 CFA call frame instruction\\n56:    DW_TAG_... = DWARF2 DIE tag */\\n57: \\n58: #include \\\"config.h\\\"\\n59: #include \\\"system.h\\\"\\n60: #include \\\"coretypes.h\\\"\\n61: #include \\\"target.h\\\"\\n62: #include \\\"function.h\\\"\\n63: #include \\\"rtl.h\\\"\\n64: #include \\\"tree.h\\\"\\n65: #include \\\"memmodel.h\\\"\\n66: #include \\\"tm_p.h\\\"\\n67: #include __sanitizer_malloc_hook(ptr, size);\\n342:   for (int i = 0; i < kMaxMallocFreeHooks; i++) {\\n343:     auto hook = MFHooks[i].malloc_hook;\\n344:     if (!hook)\\n345: \",\"endLine\":345,\"startLine\":337},{\"content\":\"367: \\n368: static int InstallMallocFreeHooks(void (*malloc_hook)(const void *, uptr),\\n369:                                   void (*free_hook)(const void *)) {\\n370:   if (!malloc_hook || !free_hook) return 0;\\n371:   for (int i = 0; i < kMaxMallocFreeHooks; i++) {\\n372:     if (MFHooks[i].malloc_hook == nullptr) {\\n373: \",\"endLine\":375,\"startLine\":367}],\"file\":\"libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common.cpp\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common.cpp\"}]}"

\",\"endLine\":41,\"startLine\":37},{\"content\":\"54: /* Write VMS debug info (using vmsdbgout.cc) and DWARF v2 debug info (using\\n55:    dwarf2out.cc).  */\\n56: #define VMS_AND_DWARF2_DEBUG \\t* except.c: #include \\\"defaults.h\\\".\\n2946: \\t(exceptions_via_longjmp): Default depends on DWARF2_UNWIND_INFO.\\n2947: \\t(emit_throw): Don't defeat assemble_external if \",\"endLine\":2951,\"startLine\":2945},{\"content\":\"2978: \\n2979: \\t* toplev.c (compile_file): Call dwarf2out_frame_{init,finish}.\\n2980: \\t* dwarf2out.c (dwarf2out_init): Don't call In practice, only\\n8: // count<int, char, long> is emitted, thanks to constant folding.\\n9: // So in theory, each of the 3 instances of count yields a\\n10: // DW_TAG_GNU_template_parameter_pack DIE, but in practise, there is only one\\n11: // DW_TAG_GNU_template_parameter_pack as there is only count<int, char, long>\\n12: // is emitted.\\n13: // { dg-final { scan-assembler-times \\\"DIE \\\\\\\\(0x\\\\[^\\\\n\\\\]*\\\\\\\\) DW_TAG_GNU_template_parameter_pack\\\" 1} }\\n14: // { dg-final { scan-assembler-times \\\"DIE \\\\\\\\(0x\\\\[^\\\\n\\\\]*\\\\\\\\) DW_TAG_template_type_param\\\" 3} }\\n15: \\n16: \",\"endLine\":16,\"startLine\":7}],\"file\":\"gcc/testsuite/g++.dg/debug/dwarf2/template-params-4.C\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/testsuite/g++.dg/debug/dwarf2/template-params-4.C\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"7: // count<char, long> and count<long>. "{\"content\":\"8000: \\n8001:   c1 = die1->die_child;\\n8002:   c2 = die2->die_child;\\n8003:   if (! c1)\\n8004:     {\\n8005:       if (c2)\\n8006: \\treturn false;\\n8007:     }\\n8008:   else\\n8009:     for (;;)\\n8010:       {\\n8011: \\tif (!same_die_p (c1, c2, mark))\\n8012: \\t  return false;\\n8013: \\tc1 = c1->die_sib;\\n8014: \\tc2 = c2->die_sib;\\n8015: \\tif (c1 == die1->die_child)\\n8016: \\t  {\\n8017: \\t    if (c2 == die2->die_child)\\n8018: \\t      break;\\n8019: \\t    else\\n8020: \\t      return false;\\n8021: \\t  }\\n8022:     }\\n8023: \\n8024:   return true;\\n8025: }\\n8026: \\n8027: /* Calculate the MD5 checksum of the compilation unit DIE UNIT_DIE and its\\n8028:    children, and set die_symbol.  */\\n8029: \\n8030: static void\\n8031: compute_comp_unit_symbol (dw_die_ref unit_die)\\n8032: {\\n8033:   const char *die_name = get_AT_string (unit_die, DW_AT_name);\\n8034:   const char *base = die_name ? lbasename (die_name) : \\\"anonymous\\\";\\n8035:   char *name = XALLOCAVEC (char, strlen (base) + 64);\\n8036:   char *p;\\n8037:   int i, mark;\\n8038:   unsigned char checksum[16];\\n8039:   struct md5_ctx ctx;\\n8040: \\n8041:   /* Compute the checksum of the DIE, then append part of it as hex digits to\\n8042:      the name filename of the unit.  */\\n8043: \\n8044:   md5_init_ctx (&ctx);\\n8045:   mark = 0;\\n8046:   die_checksum (unit_die, &ctx, &mark);\\n8047:   unmark_all_dies (unit_die);\\n8048:   md5_finish_ctx (&ctx, checksum);\\n8049: \\n8050:   /* When we this for comp_unit_die () we have a DW_AT_name that might\\n8051:      not start with a letter but with anything valid for filenames and\\n8052:      clean_symbol_name doesn't fix that up.  Prepend 'g' if the first\\n8053:      character is not a letter.  */\\n8054:   sprintf (name, \\\"%s%s.\\\", ISALPHA (*base) ? \\\"\\\" : \\\"g\\\", base);\\n8055:   clean_symbol_name (name);\\n8056: \\n8057:   p = name + strlen (name);\\n8058:   for (i = 0; i < 4; i++)\\n8059:     {\\n8060:       sprintf (p, \\\"%.2x\\\", checksum[i]);\\n8061:       p += 2;\\n8062:     }\\n8063: \\n8064:   unit_die->die_id.die_symbol = xstrdup (name);\\n8065: }\\n8066: \\n8067: /* Returns true if DIE represents a type, in the sense of TYPE_P.  */\\n8068: \\n8069: static bool\\n8070: is_type_die (dw_die_ref die)\\n8071: {\\n8072:   switch (die->die_tag)\\n8073:     {\\n8074:     case DW_TAG_array_type:\\n8075:     case DW_TAG_class_type:\\n8076:     case DW_TAG_interface_type:\\n8077:     case DW_TAG_enumeration_type:\\n8078:     case DW_TAG_pointer_type:\\n8079:     case DW_TAG_reference_type:\\n8080:     case DW_TAG_rvalue_reference_type:\\n8081:     case DW_TAG_string_type:\\n8082:     case DW_TAG_structure_type:\\n8083:     case DW_TAG_subroutine_type:\\n8084:     case DW_TAG_union_type:\\n8085:     case DW_TAG_ptr_to_member_type:\\n8086:     case DW_TAG_set_type:\\n8087:     case DW_TAG_subrange_type:\\n8088:     case DW_TAG_base_type:\\n8089:     case DW_TAG_const_type:\\n8090:     case DW_TAG_file_type:\\n8091:     case DW_TAG_packed_type:\\n8092:     case DW_TAG_volatile_type:\\n8093:     case DW_TAG_typedef:\\n8094:       return true;\\n8095:     default:\\n8096:       return false;\\n8097:     }\\n8098: }\\n8099: \\n8100: /* Returns true iff C is a compile-unit DIE.  */\\n8101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL8000-8100\"}"

"{\"content\":\"5200: /* Given a location list, remove all addresses it refers to from the\\n5201:    address_table.  */\\n5202: \\n5203: static void\\n5204: remove_loc_list_addr_table_entries (dw_loc_descr_ref descr)\\n5205: {\\n5206:   for (; descr; descr = descr->dw_loc_next)\\n5207:     if (descr->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry != NULL)\\n5208:       {\\n5209:         gcc_assert (descr->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry->index == NO_INDEX_ASSIGNED);\\n5210:         remove_addr_table_entry (descr->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry);\\n5211:       }\\n5212: }\\n5213: \\n5214: /* A helper function for dwarf2out_finish called through\\n5215:    htab_traverse.  Assign an addr_table_entry its index.  All entries\\n5216:    must be collected into the table when this function is called,\\n5217:    because the indexing code relies on htab_traverse to traverse nodes\\n5218:    in the same order for each run. */\\n5219: \\n5220: int\\n5221: index_addr_table_entry (addr_table_entry **h, unsigned int *index)\\n5222: {\\n5223:   addr_table_entry *node = *h;\\n5224: \\n5225:   /* Don't index unreferenced nodes.  */\\n5226:   if (node->refcount == 0)\\n5227:     return 1;\\n5228: \\n5229:   gcc_assert (node->index == NO_INDEX_ASSIGNED);\\n5230:   node->index = *index;\\n5231:   *index += 1;\\n5232: \\n5233:   return 1;\\n5234: }\\n5235: \\n5236: /* Return the tag of a given DIE.  */\\n5237: \\n5238: enum dwarf_tag\\n5239: dw_get_die_tag (dw_die_ref die)\\n5240: {\\n5241:   return die->die_tag;\\n5242: }\\n5243: \\n5244: /* Return a reference to the children list of a given DIE.  */\\n5245: \\n5246: dw_die_ref\\n5247: dw_get_die_child (dw_die_ref die)\\n5248: {\\n5249:   return die->die_child;\\n5250: }\\n5251: \\n5252: /* Return a reference to the sibling of a given DIE.  */\\n5253: \\n5254: dw_die_ref\\n5255: dw_get_die_sib (dw_die_ref die)\\n5256: {\\n5257:   return die->die_sib;\\n5258: }\\n5259: \\n5260: /* Add an address constant attribute value to a DIE.  When using\\n5261:    dwarf_split_debug_info, address attributes in dies destined for the\\n5262:    final executable should be direct references--setting the parameter\\n5263:    force_direct ensures this behavior.  */\\n5264: \\n5265: static inline void\\n5266: add_AT_addr (dw_die_ref die, enum dwarf_attribute attr_kind, rtx addr,\\n5267:              bool force_direct)\\n5268: {\\n5269:   dw_attr_node attr;\\n5270: \\n5271:   attr.dw_attr = attr_kind;\\n5272:   attr.dw_attr_val.val_class = dw_val_class_addr;\\n5273:   attr.dw_attr_val.v.val_addr = addr;\\n5274:   if (dwarf_split_debug_info && !force_direct)\\n5275:     attr.dw_attr_val.val_entry = add_addr_table_entry (addr, ate_kind_rtx);\\n5276:   else\\n5277:     attr.dw_attr_val.val_entry = NULL;\\n5278:   add_dwarf_attr (die, &attr);\\n5279: }\\n5280: \\n5281: /* Get the RTX from to an address DIE attribute.  */\\n5282: \\n5283: static inline rtx\\n5284: AT_addr (dw_attr_node *a)\\n5285: {\\n5286:   gcc_assert (a && AT_class (a) == dw_val_class_addr);\\n5287:   return a->dw_attr_val.v.val_addr;\\n5288: }\\n5289: \\n5290: /* Add a file attribute value to a DIE.  */\\n5291: \\n5292: static inline void\\n5293: add_AT_file (dw_die_ref die, enum dwarf_attribute attr_kind,\\n5294: \\t     struct dwarf_file_data *fd)\\n5295: {\\n5296:   dw_attr_node attr;\\n5297: \\n5298:   attr.dw_attr = attr_kind;\\n5299:   attr.dw_attr_val.val_class = dw_val_class_file;\\n5300:   attr.dw_attr_val.val_entry = NULL;\\n5301: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL5200-5300\"}"

"{\"content\":\"5600: \\n5601: static inline bool\\n5602: is_fortran (void)\\n5603: {\\n5604:   unsigned int lang = get_AT_unsigned (comp_unit_die (), DW_AT_language);\\n5605: \\n5606:   return (lang == DW_LANG_Fortran77\\n5607: \\t  || lang == DW_LANG_Fortran90\\n5608: \\t  || lang == DW_LANG_Fortran95\\n5609: \\t  || lang == DW_LANG_Fortran03\\n5610: \\t  || lang == DW_LANG_Fortran08);\\n5611: }\\n5612: \\n5613: static inline bool\\n5614: is_fortran (const_tree decl)\\n5615: {\\n5616:   if (in_lto_p)\\n5617:     {\\n5618:       const_tree context = get_ultimate_context (decl);\\n5619:       if (context && TRANSLATION_UNIT_LANGUAGE (context))\\n5620: \\treturn (strncmp (TRANSLATION_UNIT_LANGUAGE (context),\\n5621: \\t\\t\\t \\\"GNU Fortran\\\", 11) == 0\\n5622: \\t\\t|| strcmp (TRANSLATION_UNIT_LANGUAGE (context),\\n5623: \\t\\t\\t   \\\"GNU F77\\\") == 0);\\n5624:     }\\n5625:   return is_fortran ();\\n5626: }\\n5627: \\n5628: /* Return TRUE if the language is Rust.\\n5629:    Note, returns FALSE for dwarf_version < 5 && dwarf_strict. */\\n5630: \\n5631: static inline bool\\n5632: is_rust (void)\\n5633: {\\n5634:   unsigned int lang = get_AT_unsigned (comp_unit_die (), DW_AT_language);\\n5635: \\n5636:   return lang == DW_LANG_Rust;\\n5637: }\\n5638: \\n5639: /* Return TRUE if the language is Ada.  */\\n5640: \\n5641: static inline bool\\n5642: is_ada (void)\\n5643: {\\n5644:   unsigned int lang = get_AT_unsigned (comp_unit_die (), DW_AT_language);\\n5645: \\n5646:   return lang == DW_LANG_Ada95 || lang == DW_LANG_Ada83;\\n5647: }\\n5648: \\n5649: /* Return TRUE if the language is D.  */\\n5650: \\n5651: static inline bool\\n5652: is_dlang (void)\\n5653: {\\n5654:   unsigned int lang = get_AT_unsigned (comp_unit_die (), DW_AT_language);\\n5655: \\n5656:   return lang == DW_LANG_D;\\n5657: }\\n5658: \\n5659: /* Remove the specified attribute if present.  Return TRUE if removal\\n5660:    was successful.  */\\n5661: \\n5662: static bool\\n5663: remove_AT (dw_die_ref die, enum dwarf_attribute attr_kind)\\n5664: {\\n5665:   dw_attr_node *a;\\n5666:   unsigned ix;\\n5667: \\n5668:   if (! die)\\n5669:     return false;\\n5670: \\n5671:   FOR_EACH_VEC_SAFE_ELT (die->die_attr, ix, a)\\n5672:     if (a->dw_attr == attr_kind)\\n5673:       {\\n5674: \\tif (AT_class (a) == dw_val_class_str)\\n5675: \\t  if (a->dw_attr_val.v.val_str->refcount)\\n5676: \\t    a->dw_attr_val.v.val_str->refcount--;\\n5677: \\n5678: \\t/* vec::ordered_remove should help reduce the number of abbrevs\\n5679: \\t   that are needed.  */\\n5680: \\tdie->die_attr->ordered_remove (ix);\\n5681: \\treturn true;\\n5682:       }\\n5683:   return false;\\n5684: }\\n5685: \\n5686: /* Remove CHILD from its parent.  PREV must have the property that\\n5687:    PREV->DIE_SIB == CHILD.  Does not alter CHILD.  */\\n5688: \\n5689: static void\\n5690: remove_child_with_prev (dw_die_ref child, dw_die_ref prev)\\n5691: {\\n5692:   gcc_assert (child->die_parent == prev->die_parent);\\n5693:   gcc_assert (prev->die_sib == child);\\n5694:   if (prev == child)\\n5695:     {\\n5696:       gcc_assert (child->die_parent->die_child == child);\\n5697:       prev = NULL;\\n5698:     }\\n5699:   else\\n5700:     prev->die_sib = child->die_sib;\\n5701: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL5600-5700\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"5700:     prev->die_sib = child->die_sib;\\n5701:   if (child->die_parent->die_child == child)\\n5702:     child->die_parent->die_child = prev;\\n5703:   child->die_sib = NULL;\\n5704: }\\n5705: \\n5706: /* Replace OLD_CHILD with NEW_CHILD.  PREV must have the property that\\n5707:    PREV->DIE_SIB == OLD_CHILD.  Does not alter OLD_CHILD.  */\\n5708: \\n5709: static void\\n5710: replace_child (dw_die_ref old_child, dw_die_ref new_child, dw_die_ref prev)\\n5711: {\\n5712:   dw_die_ref parent = old_child->die_parent;\\n5713: \\n5714:   gcc_assert (parent == prev->die_parent);\\n5715:   gcc_assert (prev->die_sib == old_child);\\n5716: \\n5717:   new_child->die_parent = parent;\\n5718:   if (prev == old_child)\\n5719:     {\\n5720:       gcc_assert (parent->die_child == old_child);\\n5721:       new_child->die_sib = new_child;\\n5722:     }\\n5723:   else\\n5724:     {\\n5725:       prev->die_sib = new_child;\\n5726:       new_child->die_sib = old_child->die_sib;\\n5727:     }\\n5728:   if (old_child->die_parent->die_child == old_child)\\n5729:     old_child->die_parent->die_child = new_child;\\n5730:   old_child->die_sib = NULL;\\n5731: }\\n5732: \\n5733: /* Move all children from OLD_PARENT to NEW_PARENT.  */\\n5734: \\n5735: static void\\n5736: move_all_children (dw_die_ref old_parent, dw_die_ref new_parent)\\n5737: {\\n5738:   dw_die_ref c;\\n5739:   new_parent->die_child = old_parent->die_child;\\n5740:   old_parent->die_child = NULL;\\n5741:   FOR_EACH_CHILD (new_parent, c, c->die_parent = new_parent);\\n5742: }\\n5743: \\n5744: /* Remove child DIE whose die_tag is TAG.  Do nothing if no child\\n5745:    matches TAG.  */\\n5746: \\n5747: static void\\n5748: remove_child_TAG (dw_die_ref die, enum dwarf_tag tag)\\n5749: {\\n5750:   dw_die_ref c;\\n5751: \\n5752:   c = die->die_child;\\n5753:   if (c) do {\\n5754:     dw_die_ref prev = c;\\n5755:     c = c->die_sib;\\n5756:     while (c->die_tag == tag)\\n5757:       {\\n5758: \\tremove_child_with_prev (c, prev);\\n5759: \\tc->die_parent = NULL;\\n5760: \\t/* Might have removed every child.  */\\n5761: \\tif (die->die_child == NULL)\\n5762: \\t  return;\\n5763: \\tc = prev->die_sib;\\n5764:       }\\n5765:   } while (c != die->die_child);\\n5766: }\\n5767: \\n5768: /* Add a CHILD_DIE as the last child of DIE.  */\\n5769: \\n5770: static void\\n5771: add_child_die (dw_die_ref die, dw_die_ref child_die)\\n5772: {\\n5773:   /* FIXME this should probably be an assert.  */\\n5774:   if (! die || ! child_die)\\n5775:     return;\\n5776:   gcc_assert (die != child_die);\\n5777: \\n5778:   child_die->die_parent = die;\\n5779:   if (die->die_child)\\n5780:     {\\n5781:       child_die->die_sib = die->die_child->die_sib;\\n5782:       die->die_child->die_sib = child_die;\\n5783:     }\\n5784:   else\\n5785:     child_die->die_sib = child_die;\\n5786:   die->die_child = child_die;\\n5787: }\\n5788: \\n5789: /* Like add_child_die, but put CHILD_DIE after AFTER_DIE.  */\\n5790: \\n5791: static void\\n5792: add_child_die_after (dw_die_ref die, dw_die_ref child_die,\\n5793: \\t\\t     dw_die_ref after_die)\\n5794: {\\n5795:   gcc_assert (die\\n5796: \\t      && child_die\\n5797: \\t      && after_die\\n5798: \\t      && die->die_child\\n5799: \\t      && die != child_die);\\n5800: \\n5801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL5700-5800\"}"
