void\\n119: initialize_vtbl_ptrs (tree addr)\\n120: {\\n121: \",\"endLine\":121,\"startLine\":118},{\"content\":\"814: struct find_uninit_data {\\n815:   /* The set tracking the yet-uninitialized members.  */\\n816:   hash_set<tree> *uninitialized;\\n817:   /* The data member we are currently initializing.  It can be either\\n818:      a type (initializing a base class/delegating constructors), or\\n819:      a COMPONENT_REF.  */\\n820: \",\"endLine\":820,\"startLine\":814},{\"content\":\"1806: static tree\\n1807: initializing_context (tree field)\\n1808: {\\n1809: \",\"endLine\":1809,\"startLine\":1806}],\"file\":\"gcc/cp/init.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/cp/init.cc\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"25: \\n26: --  This package contains definitions for initial sizes and growth increments\\n27: --  for the various dynamic arrays used for the main compiler data structures.\\n28: --  The indicated initial size is allocated for the start of each file, and\\n29: --  the increment factor is a percentage used to increase the table size when\\n30: \",\"endLine\":30,\"startLine\":25},{\"content\":\"78: \\n79:    Linker_Option_Lines_Initial      : constant := 5;          -- Lib\\n80:    Linker_Option_Lines_Increment    : constant := 200;\\n81: \",\"endLine\":81,\"startLine\":78},{\"content\":\"141: \\n142:    Units_Initial                    : constant := 30;         -- Lib\\n143:    Units_Increment                  : constant := 100;\\n144: \",\"endLine\":144,\"startLine\":141}],\"file\":\"gcc/ada/alloc.ads\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/alloc.ads\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1437: \t(abbrev_label, debug_info_label, debug_line_label, text_label): New.\n1438: \t(dwarf2out_init): Initialize the vars.  Output defn for text_label.\n1439: \t(dwarf2out_finish): Output defns for the other 3 vars.\n1440: \t(dw_val_node): Rename val_section to val_section_label, as it's\n1441: ",
            "endLine": 1441,
            "startLine": 1437
          },
          {
            "content": "800: \t(pedwarn_with_file_and_line, fatal): Likewise.\n801: \t(initialize_char_syntax): No need to initialize char_name.\n802: \t* cexp.y (yyerror): Now takes msgid format and args, not just string.\n803: ",
            "endLine": 803,
            "startLine": 800
          },
          {
            "content": "1730: \t* rs6000.h (ADDITIONAL_REGISTER_NAMES): Add missing braces around\n1731: \tinitializer.\n1732: \t(get_issue_rate, non_logical_cint_operand): Add prototype.\n1733: ",
            "endLine": 1733,
            "startLine": 1730
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/FSFChangeLog",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/FSFChangeLog"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "107: static vec<unsigned> funcnum_table;\n108: #define FUNC_TABLE_INITIAL 256\n109: \n110: /* Local pointer to the name of the main input file.  Initialized in\n111:    vmsdbgout_init.  */\n112: ",
            "endLine": 112,
            "startLine": 107
          },
          {
            "content": "1449:   if (write_symbols == VMS_AND_DWARF2_DEBUG)\n1450:     (*dwarf2_debug_hooks.init) (filename);\n1451: \n1452: ",
            "endLine": 1452,
            "startLine": 1449
          },
          {
            "content": "1580:   if (write_symbols == VMS_AND_DWARF2_DEBUG)\n1581:     (*dwarf2_debug_hooks.finish) (filename);\n1582: \n1583: ",
            "endLine": 1583,
            "startLine": 1580
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/vmsdbgout.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/vmsdbgout.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "91:         * dwarf2out.c: Include input.h.\n92:         (dwarf2_debug_hooks): Add new hook for imported_module_or_decl.\n93:         (remove_child_TAG): New function.\n94: ",
            "endLine": 94,
            "startLine": 91
          },
          {
            "content": "95:         (gen_subprogram_die): Equate decl number to declaration die. Do not\n96:         remove all children dies while reusing declaration die for definition.\n97:         Instead, selectively remove only formal parameters.\n98: ",
            "endLine": 98,
            "startLine": 95
          }
        ],
        "file": "contrib/reghunt/examples/29906a.log",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/contrib/reghunt/examples/29906a.log"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "256: \t* m68k/mot3300.h (ASM_BYTE_OP): Don't include '\\t' in the\n257: \tdefinition.\n258: \t(ASM_OUTPUT_ASCII): Prefix ASM_BYTE_OP by one single '\\t'.\n259: ",
            "endLine": 259,
            "startLine": 256
          },
          {
            "content": "1005: \tTARGET_ENDIAN_DEFAULT == zero instead of testing for macro\n1006: \tdefinition.\n1007: \n1008: ",
            "endLine": 1008,
            "startLine": 1005
          },
          {
            "content": "664: \t(new_eh_context, __get_eh_context,\n665: \teh_pthread_initialize, eh_context_initialize, eh_context_static,\n666: \teh_context_specific, eh_context_free): New functions.\n667: ",
            "endLine": 667,
            "startLine": 664
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ChangeLog-1997",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ChangeLog-1997"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "242: extern const struct gcc_debug_hooks xcoff_debug_hooks;\n243: extern const struct gcc_debug_hooks dwarf2_debug_hooks;\n244: extern const struct gcc_debug_hooks dwarf2_lineno_debug_hooks;\n245: ",
            "endLine": 245,
            "startLine": 242
          },
          {
            "content": "29: {\n30:   /* Initialize debug output.  MAIN_FILENAME is the name of the main\n31:      input file.  */\n32: ",
            "endLine": 32,
            "startLine": 29
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/debug.h",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/debug.h"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1870: void\n1871: initialize_rtl (void)\n1872: {\n1873:   auto_timevar tv (g_timer, TV_INITIALIZE_RTL);\n1874: \n1875:   /* Initialization done just once per compilation, but delayed\n1876:      till code generation.  */\n1877:   if (!rtl_initialized)\n1878:     ira_init_once ();\n1879:   rtl_initialized = true;\n1880: \n1881:   /* Target specific RTL backend initialization.  */\n1882:   if (!this_target_rtl->target_specific_initialized)\n1883:     {\n1884: ",
            "endLine": 1884,
            "startLine": 1870
          },
          {
            "content": "1867: \n1868: static int rtl_initialized;\n1869: \n1870: ",
            "endLine": 1870,
            "startLine": 1867
          },
          {
            "content": "2168: \t command line options, but before the parsers are\n2169: \t initialized.  */\n2170:       for (i = 0; i < NUM_INT_N_ENTS; i ++)\n2171: ",
            "endLine": 2171,
            "startLine": 2168
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/toplev.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/toplev.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1454: \t  /* The .maps section has special meaning in BTF: it is used for BPF\n1455: \t     map definitions.  These definitions should be structs.  We must\n1456: \t     collect pointee types used in map members as though they are used\n1457: ",
            "endLine": 1457,
            "startLine": 1454
          },
          {
            "content": "271: \n272: /* Initialize BTF section (.BTF) for output.  */\n273: \n274: ",
            "endLine": 274,
            "startLine": 271
          },
          {
            "content": "183:   if (kind == BTF_KIND_INT && dtd->dtd_data.ctti_size == 0)\n184:     /* This is a (redundant) definition of void.  */\n185:     return false;\n186: ",
            "endLine": 186,
            "startLine": 183
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/btfout.cc",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/btfout.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2798: \t(update_eliminable_offsets): Don't compute it.\n2799: \t(set_initial_elim_offsets): Don't initialize it.\n2800: \tBreak out some code into set_initial_label_offsets so the rest of\n2801: \tthis function can be called from reload_as_needed.\n2802: \tAssume that INITIAL_FRAME_POINTER_OFFSET is defined when\n2803: \tELIMINABLE_REGS isn't.\n2804: \t(set_initial_label_offsets): New function, broken out of\n2805: \tset_initial_elim_offsets.\n2806: \t(set_offsets_for_label): New function, broken out of set_label_offsets\n2807: ",
            "endLine": 2807,
            "startLine": 2798
          },
          {
            "content": "1320: \tLONG_FLOAT_REGO is likely spilled but not caught by default\n1321: \tdefinition.\n1322: \t(CONSTANT_ADDRESS_P, CONSTANT_ADDRESS_NO_LABEL_P): Use macro\n1323: ",
            "endLine": 1323,
            "startLine": 1320
          },
          {
            "content": "11884: \t(cpp_warning_with_line): Use it.  Accept variable arguments.  Hide\n11885: \tdefinition.\n11886: \t(cpp_pedwarn_with_line): Accept variable arguments.\n11887: ",
            "endLine": 11887,
            "startLine": 11884
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ChangeLog-1998",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ChangeLog-1998"
      },

        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/cp/init.cc"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "25: \n26: --  This package contains definitions for initial sizes and growth increments\n27: --  for the various dynamic arrays used for the main compiler data structures.\n28: --  The indicated initial size is allocated for the start of each file, and\n29: --  the increment factor is a percentage used to increase the table size when\n30: ",
            "endLine": 30,
            "startLine": 25
          },
          {
            "content": "78: \n79:    Linker_Option_Lines_Initial      : constant := 5;          -- Lib\n80:    Linker_Option_Lines_Increment    : constant := 200;\n81: ",
            "endLine": 81,
            "startLine": 78
          },
          {
            "content": "141: \n142:    Units_Initial                    : constant := 30;         -- Lib\n143:    Units_Increment                  : constant := 100;\n144: ",
            "endLine": 144,
            "startLine": 141
          }
        ],
        "file": "gcc/ada/alloc.ads",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333/-/blob/gcc/ada/alloc.ads"
      }
    ]
  }
}

DECL.\\n28046:    NAME is non-NULL name in context if the decl has been renamed.\\n28047:    CHILD is true if decl is one of the renamed decls as part of\\n28048:    importing whole module.\\n28049: name, tree context,\\n28054: \\t\\t\\t\\t   bool child, bool implicit)\\n28055: {\\n28056:   /* dw_die_ref at_import_die;  */\\n28057:   dw_die_ref scope_die;\\n28058: \\n28059:   if (debug_info_level   "content": "{\"content\":\"28400:   if (!tmpl_value_parm_die_table)\\n28401:     vec_alloc (tmpl_value_parm_die_table, 32);\\n28402: \\n28403:   entry.die = die;\\n28404:   entry.arg = arg;\\n28405:   vec_safe_push (tmpl_value_parm_die_table, entry);\\n28406: }\\n28407: \\n28408: /* Return TRUE if T is an instance of generic type, FALSE\\n28409:    otherwise.  */\\n28410: \\n28411: static bool\\n28412: generic_type_p (tree t)\\n28413: {\\n28414:   if (t == NULL_TREE || !TYPE_P (t))\\n28415:     return false;\\n28416:   return lang_hooks.get_innermost_generic_parms (t) != NULL_TREE;\\n28417: }\\n28418: \\n28419: /* Schedule the generation of the generic parameter dies for the\\n28420:   instance of generic type T. The proper generation itself is later\\n28421:   done by gen_scheduled_generic_parms_dies. */\\n28422: \\n28423: static void\\n28424: schedule_generic_params_dies_gen (tree t)\\n28425: {\\n28426:   if (!generic_type_p (t))\\n28427:     return;\\n28428: \\n28429:   gcc_assert (early_dwarf);\\n28430: \\n28431:   if (!generic_type_instances)\\n28432:     vec_alloc (generic_type_instances, 256);\\n28433: \\n28434:   vec_safe_push (generic_type_instances, t);\\n28435: }\\n28436: \\n28437: /* Add a DW_AT_const_value attribute to DIEs that were scheduled\\n28438:    by append_entry_to_tmpl_value_parm_die_table. This function must\\n28439:    be called after function DIEs have been generated.  */\\n28440: \\n28441: static void\\n28442: gen_remaining_tmpl_value_param_die_attribute (void)\\n28443: {\\n28444:   if (tmpl_value_parm_die_table)\\n28445:     {\\n28446:       unsigned i, j;\\n28447:       die_arg_entry *e;\\n28448: \\n28449:       /* We do this in two phases - first get the cases we can\\n28450: \\t handle during early-finish, preserving those we cannot\\n28451: \\t (containing symbolic constants where we don't yet know\\n28452: \\t whether we are going to output the referenced symbols).\\n28453: \\t For those we try again at late-finish.  */\\n28454:       j = 0;\\n28455:       FOR_EACH_VEC_ELT (*tmpl_value_parm_die_table, i, e)\\n28456: \\t{\\n28457: \\t  if (!e->die->removed\\n28458: \\t      && !tree_add_const_value_attribute (e->die, e->arg))\\n28459: \\t    {\\n28460: \\t      dw_loc_descr_ref loc = NULL;\\n28461: \\t      if (! early_dwarf\\n28462: \\t\\t  && (dwarf_version >= 5 || !dwarf_strict))\\n28463: \\t\\tloc = loc_descriptor_from_tree (e->arg, 2, NULL);\\n28464: \\t      if (loc)\\n28465: \\t\\tadd_AT_loc (e->die, DW_AT_location, loc);\\n28466: \\t      else\\n28467: \\t\\t(*tmpl_value_parm_die_table)[j++] = *e;\\n28468: \\t    }\\n28469: \\t}\\n28470:       tmpl_value_parm_die_table->truncate (j);\\n28471:     }\\n28472: }\\n28473: \\n28474: /* Generate generic parameters DIEs for instances of generic types\\n28475:    that have been previously scheduled by\\n28476:    schedule_generic_params_dies_gen. This function must be called\\n28477:    after all the types of the CU have been laid out.  */\\n28478: \\n28479: static void\\n28480: gen_scheduled_generic_parms_dies (void)\\n28481: {\\n28482:   unsigned i;\\n28483:   tree t;\\n28484: \\n28485:   if (!generic_type_instances)\\n28486:     return;\\n28487: \\n28488:   FOR_EACH_VEC_ELT (*generic_type_instances, i, t)\\n28489:     if (COMPLETE_TYPE_P (t))\\n28490:       gen_generic_params_dies (t);\\n28491: \\n28492:   generic_type_instances = NULL;\\n28493: }\\n28494: \\n28495: \\n28496: /* Replace DW_AT_name for the decl with name.  */\\n28497: \\n28498: static void\\n28499: dwarf2out_set_name (tree decl, tree name)\\n28500: {\\n28501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL28400-28500\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "28400:   if (!tmpl_value_parm_die_table)\n28401:     vec_alloc (tmpl_value_parm_die_table, 32);\n28402: \n28403:   entry.die = die;\n28404:   entry.arg = arg;\n28405:   vec_safe_push (tmpl_value_parm_die_table, entry);\n28406: }\n28407: \n28408: /* Return TRUE if T is an instance of generic type, FALSE\n28409:    otherwise.  */\n28410: \n28411: static bool\n28412: generic_type_p (tree t)\n28413: {\n28414:   if (t == NULL_TREE || !TYPE_P (t))\n28415:     return false;\n28416:   return lang_hooks.get_innermost_generic_parms (t) != NULL_TREE;\n28417: }\n28418: \n28419: /* Schedule the generation of the generic parameter dies for the\n28420:   instance of generic type T. The proper generation itself is later\n28421:   done by gen_scheduled_generic_parms_dies. */\n28422: \n28423: static void\n28424: schedule_generic_params_dies_gen (tree t)\n28425: {\n28426:   if (!generic_type_p (t))\n28427:     return;\n28428: \n28429:   gcc_assert (early_dwarf);\n28430: \n28431:   if (!generic_type_instances)\n28432:     vec_alloc (generic_type_instances, 256);\n28433: \n28434:   vec_safe_push (generic_type_instances, t);\n28435: }\n28436: \n28437: /* Add a DW_AT_const_value attribute to DIEs that were scheduled\n28438:    by append_entry_to_tmpl_value_parm_die_table. This function must\n28439:    be called after function DIEs have been generated.  */\n28440: \n28441: static void\n28442: gen_remaining_tmpl_value_param_die_attribute (void)\n28443: {\n28444:   if (tmpl_value_parm_die_table)\n28445:     {\n28446:       unsigned i, j;\n28447:       die_arg_entry *e;\n28448: \n28449:       /* We do this in two phases - first get the cases we can\n28450: \t handle during early-finish, preserving those we cannot\n28451: \t (containing symbolic constants where we don't yet know\n28452: \t whether we are going to output the referenced symbols).\n28453: \t For those we try again at late-finish.  */\n28454:       j = 0;\n28455:       FOR_EACH_VEC_ELT (*tmpl_value_parm_die_table, i, e)\n28456: \t{\n28457: \t  if (!e->die->removed\n28458: \t      && !tree_add_const_value_attribute (e->die, e->arg))\n28459: \t    {\n28460: \t      dw_loc_descr_ref loc = NULL;\n28461: \t      if (! early_dwarf\n28462: \t\t  && (dwarf_version >= 5 || !dwarf_strict))\n28463: \t\tloc = loc_descriptor_from_tree (e->arg, 2, NULL);\n28464: \t      if (loc)\n28465: \t\tadd_AT_loc (e->die, DW_AT_location, loc);\n28466: \t      else\n28467: \t\t(*tmpl_value_parm_die_table)[j++] = *e;\n28468: \t    }\n28469: \t}\n28470:       tmpl_value_parm_die_table->truncate (j);\n28471:     }\n28472: }\n28473: \n28474: /* Generate generic parameters DIEs for instances of generic types\n28475:    that have been previously scheduled by\n28476:    schedule_generic_params_dies_gen. This function must be called\n28477:    after all the types of the CU have been laid out.  */\n28478: \n28479: static void\n28480: gen_scheduled_generic_parms_dies (void)\n28481: {\n28482:   unsigned i;\n28483:   tree t;\n28484: \n28485:   if (!generic_type_instances)\n28486:     return;\n28487: \n28488:   FOR_EACH_VEC_ELT (*generic_type_instances, i, t)\n28489:     if (COMPLETE_TYPE_P (t))\n28490:       gen_generic_params_dies (t);\n28491: \n28492:   generic_type_instances = NULL;\n28493: }\n28494: \n28495: \n28496: /* Replace DW_AT_name for the decl with name.  */\n28497: \n28498: static void\n28499: dwarf2out_set_name (tree decl, tree name)\n28500: {\n28501: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL28400-28500"
  }
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"28500: {\\n28501:   dw_die_ref die;\\n28502:   dw_attr_node *attr;\\n28503:   const char *dname;\\n28504: \\n28505:   die = TYPE_SYMTAB_DIE (decl);\\n28506:   if (!die)\\n28507:     return;\\n28508: \\n28509:   dname = dwarf2_name (name, 0);\\n28510:   if (!dname)\\n28511:     return;\\n28512: \\n28513:   attr = get_AT (die, DW_AT_name);\\n28514:   if (attr)\\n28515:     {\\n28516:       struct indirect_string_node *node;\\n28517: \\n28518:       node = find_AT_string (dname);\\n28519:       /* replace the string.  */\\n28520:       attr->dw_attr_val.v.val_str = node;\\n28521:     }\\n28522: \\n28523:   else\\n28524:     add_name_attribute (die, dname);\\n28525: }\\n28526: \\n28527: /* True if before or during processing of the first function being emitted.  */\\n28528: static bool in_first_function_p = true;\\n28529: /* True if loc_note during dwarf2out_var_location call might still be\\n28530:    before first real instruction at address equal to .Ltext0.  */\\n28531: static bool maybe_at_text_label_p = true;\\n28532: /* One above highest N where .LVLN label might be equal to .Ltext0 label.  */\\n28533: static unsigned int first_loclabel_num_not_at_text_label;\\n28534: \\n28535: /* Look ahead for a real insn.  */\\n28536: \\n28537: static rtx_insn *\\n28538: dwarf2out_next_real_insn (rtx_insn *loc_note)\\n28539: {\\n28540:   rtx_insn *next_real = NEXT_INSN (loc_note);\\n28541: \\n28542:   while (next_real)\\n28543:     if (INSN_P (next_real))\\n28544:       break;\\n28545:     else\\n28546:       next_real = NEXT_INSN (next_real);\\n28547: \\n28548:   return next_real;\\n28549: }\\n28550: \\n28551: /* Called by the final INSN scan whenever we see a var location.  We\\n28552:    use it to drop labels in the right places, and throw the location in\\n28553:    our lookup table.  */\\n28554: \\n28555: static void\\n28556: dwarf2out_var_location (rtx_insn *loc_note)\\n28557: {\\n28558:   char loclabel[MAX_ARTIFICIAL_LABEL_BYTES + 2];\\n28559:   struct var_loc_node *newloc;\\n28560:   rtx_insn *next_real;\\n28561:   rtx_insn *call_insn = NULL;\\n28562:   static const char *last_label;\\n28563:   static const char *last_postcall_label;\\n28564:   static bool last_in_cold_section_p;\\n28565:   static rtx_insn *expected_next_loc_note;\\n28566:   tree decl;\\n28567:   bool var_loc_p;\\n28568:   var_loc_view view = 0;\\n28569: \\n28570:   if (!NOTE_P (loc_note))\\n28571:     {\\n28572:       if (CALL_P (loc_note))\\n28573: \\t{\\n28574: \\t  maybe_reset_location_view (loc_note, cur_line_info_table);\\n28575: \\t  call_site_count++;\\n28576: \\t  if (SIBLING_CALL_P (loc_note))\\n28577: \\t    tail_call_site_count++;\\n28578: \\t  if (find_reg_note (loc_note, REG_CALL_ARG_LOCATION, NULL_RTX))\\n28579: \\t    {\\n28580: \\t      call_insn = loc_note;\\n28581: \\t      loc_note = NULL;\\n28582: \\t      var_loc_p = false;\\n28583: \\n28584: \\t      next_real = dwarf2out_next_real_insn (call_insn);\\n28585: \\t      cached_next_real_insn = NULL;\\n28586: \\t      goto create_label;\\n28587: \\t    }\\n28588: \\t  if (optimize == 0 && !flag_var_tracking)\\n28589: \\t    {\\n28590: \\t      /* When the var-tracking pass is not running, there is no note\\n28591: \\t\\t for indirect calls whose target is compile-time known. In this\\n28592: \\t\\t case, process such calls specifically so that we generate call\\n28593: \\t\\t sites for them anyway.  */\\n28594: \\t      rtx x = PATTERN (loc_note);\\n28595: \\t      if (GET_CODE (x) == PARALLEL)\\n28596: \\t\\tx = XVECEXP (x, 0, 0);\\n28597: \\t      if (GET_CODE (x) == SET)\\n28598: \\t\\tx = SET_SRC (x);\\n28599: \\t      if (GET_CODE (x) == CALL)\\n28600: \\t\\tx = XEXP (x, 0);\\n28601: \\t      if (!MEM_P (x)\\n28602: \\t\\t  || GET_CODE (XEXP (x, 0)) != SYMBOL_REF\\n28603: \\t\\t  || !SYMBOL_REF_DECL (XEXP (x, 0))\\n28604: \\t\\t  || (TREE_CODE (SYMBOL_REF_DECL (XEXP (x, 0)))\\n28605: \\t\\t      != FUNCTION_DECL))\\n28606: \\t\\t{\\n28607: \\t\\t  call_insn = loc_note;\\n28608: \\t\\t  loc_note = NULL;\\n28609: \\t\\t  var_loc_p = false;\\n28610: \\n28611: \\t\\t  next_real = dwarf2out_next_real_insn (call_insn);\\n28612: \\t\\t  cached_next_real_insn = NULL;\\n28613: \\t\\t  goto create_label;\\n28614: \\t\\t}\\n28615: \\t    }\\n28616: \\t}\\n28617:       else if (!debug_variable_location_views)\\n28618: \\tgcc_unreachable ();\\n28619:       else\\n28620: \\tmaybe_reset_location_view (loc_note, cur_line_info_table);\\n28621: \\n28622:       return;\\n28623:     }\\n28624: \\n28625:   var_loc_p = NOTE_KIND (loc_note) == NOTE_INSN_VAR_LOCATION;\\n28626:   if (var_loc_p && !DECL_P (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_DECL (loc_note)))\\n28627:     return;\\n28628: \\n28629:   /* Optimize processing a large consecutive sequence of location\\n28630:      notes so we don't spend too much time in next_real_insn.  If the\\n28631:      next insn is another location note, remember the next_real_insn\\n28632:      calculation for next time.  */\\n28633:   next_real = cached_next_real_insn;\\n28634:   if (next_real)\\n28635:     {\\n28636:       if (expected_next_loc_note != loc_note)\\n28637: \\tnext_real = NULL;\\n28638:     }\\n28639: \\n28640:   if (! next_real)\\n28641:     next_real = dwarf2out_next_real_insn (loc_note);\\n28642: \\n28643:   if (next_real)\\n28644:     {\\n28645:       rtx_insn *next_note = NEXT_INSN (loc_note);\\n28646:       while (next_note != next_real)\\n28647: \\t{\\n28648: \\t  if (! next_note->deleted ()\\n28649: \\t      && NOTE_P (next_note)\\n28650: \\t      && NOTE_KIND (next_note) == NOTE_INSN_VAR_LOCATION)\\n28651: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL28500-28650\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "28500: {\n28501:   dw_die_ref die;\n28502:   dw_attr_node *attr;\n28503:   const char *dname;\n28504: \n28505:   die = TYPE_SYMTAB_DIE (decl);\n28506:   if (!die)\n28507:     return;\n28508: \n28509:   dname = dwarf2_name (name, 0);\n28510:   if (!dname)\n28511:     return;\n28512: \n28513:   attr = get_AT (die, DW_AT_name);\n28514:   if (attr)\n28515:     {\n28516:       struct indirect_string_node *node;\n28517: \n28518:       node = find_AT_string (dname);\n28519:       /* replace the string.  */\n28520:       attr->dw_attr_val.v.val_str = node;\n28521:     }\n28522: \n28523:   else\n28524:     add_name_attribute (die, dname);\n28525: }\n28526: \n28527: /* True if before or during processing of the first function being emitted.  */\n28528: static bool in_first_function_p = true;\n28529: /* True if loc_note during dwarf2out_var_location call might still be\n28530:    before first real instruction at address equal to .Ltext0.  */\n28531: static bool maybe_at_text_label_p = true;\n28532: /* One above highest N where .LVLN label might be equal to .Ltext0 label.  */\n28533: static unsigned int first_loclabel_num_not_at_text_label;\n28534: \n28535: /* Look ahead for a real insn.  */\n28536: \n28537: static rtx_insn *\n28538: dwarf2out_next_real_insn (rtx_insn *loc_note)\n28539: {\n28540:   rtx_insn *next_real = NEXT_INSN (loc_note);\n28541: \n28542:   while (next_real)\n28543:     if (INSN_P (next_real))\n28544:       break;\n28545:     else\n28546:       next_real = NEXT_INSN (next_real);\n28547: \n28548:   return next_real;\n28549: }\n28550: \n28551: /* Called by the final INSN scan whenever we see a var location.  We\n28552:    use it to drop labels in the right places, and throw the location in\n28553:    our lookup table.  */\n28554: \n28555: static void\n28556: dwarf2out_var_location (rtx_insn *loc_note)\n28557: {\n28558:   char loclabel[MAX_ARTIFICIAL_LABEL_BYTES + 2];\n28559:   struct var_loc_node *newloc;\n28560:   rtx_insn *next_real;\n28561:   rtx_insn *call_insn = NULL;\n28562:   static const char *last_label;\n28563:   static const char *last_postcall_label;\n28564:   static bool last_in_cold_section_p;\n28565:   static rtx_insn *expected_next_loc_note;\n28566:   tree decl;\n28567:   bool var_loc_p;\n28568:   var_loc_view view = 0;\n28569: \n28570:   if (!NOTE_P (loc_note))\n28571:     {\n28572:       if (CALL_P (loc_note))\n28573: \t{\n28574: \t  maybe_reset_location_view (loc_note, cur_line_info_table);\n28575: \t  call_site_count++;\n28576: \t  if (SIBLING_CALL_P (loc_note))\n28577: \t    tail_call_site_count++;\n28578: \t  if (find_reg_note (loc_note, REG_CALL_ARG_LOCATION, NULL_RTX))\n28579: \t    {\n28580: \t      call_insn = loc_note;\n28581: \t      loc_note = NULL;\n28582: \t      var_loc_p = false;\n28583: \n28584: \t      next_real = dwarf2out_next_real_insn (call_insn);\n28585: \t      cached_next_real_insn = NULL;\n28586: \t      goto create_label;\n28587: \t    }\n28588: \t  if (optimize == 0 && !flag_var_tracking)\n28589: \t    {\n28590: \t      /* When the var-tracking pass is not running, there is no note\n28591: \t\t for indirect calls whose target is compile-time known. In this\n28592: \t\t case, process such calls specifically so that we generate call\n28593: \t\t sites for them anyway.  */\n28594: \t      rtx x = PATTERN (loc_note);\n28595: \t      if (GET_CODE (x) == PARALLEL)\n28596: \t\tx = XVECEXP (x, 0, 0);\n28597: \t      if (GET_CODE (x) == SET)\n28598: \t\tx = SET_SRC (x);\n28599: \t      if (GET_CODE (x) == CALL)\n28600: \t\tx = XEXP (x, 0);\n28601: \t      if (!MEM_P (x)\n28602: \t\t  || GET_CODE (XEXP (x, 0)) != SYMBOL_REF\n28603: \t\t  || !SYMBOL_REF_DECL (XEXP (x, 0))\n28604: \t\t  || (TREE_CODE (SYMBOL_REF_DECL (XEXP (x, 0)))\n28605: \t\t      != FUNCTION_DECL))\n28606: \t\t{\n28607: \t\t  call_insn = loc_note;\n28608: \t\t  loc_note = NULL;\n28609: \t\t  var_loc_p = false;\n28610: \n28611: \t\t  next_real = dwarf2out_next_real_insn (call_insn);\n28612: \t\t  cached_next_real_insn = NULL;\n28613: \t\t  goto create_label;\n28614: \t\t}\n28615: \t    }\n28616: \t}\n28617:       else if (!debug_variable_location_views)\n28618: \tgcc_unreachable ();\n28619:       else\n28620: \tmaybe_reset_location_view (loc_note, cur_line_info_table);\n28621: \n28622:       return;\n28623:     }\n28624: \n28625:   var_loc_p = NOTE_KIND (loc_note) == NOTE_INSN_VAR_LOCATION;\n28626:   if (var_loc_p && !DECL_P (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_DECL (loc_note)))\n28627:     return;\n28628: \n28629:   /* Optimize processing a large consecutive sequence of location\n28630:      notes so we don't spend too much time in next_real_insn. 96. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"28800: \\tcall_arg_locations = ca_loc;\\n28801:       call_arg_loc_last = ca_loc;\\n28802:     }\\n28803:   else if (loc_note != NULL_RTX && !NOTE_DURING_CALL_P (loc_note))\\n28804:     {\\n28805:       newloc->label = last_label;\\n28806:       newloc->view = view;\\n28807:     }\\n28808:   else\\n28809:     {\\n28810:       if (!last_postcall_label)\\n28811: \\t{\\n28812: \\t  sprintf (loclabel, \\\"%s-1\\\", last_label);\\n28813: \\t  last_postcall_label = ggc_strdup (loclabel);\\n28814: \\t}\\n28815:       newloc->label = last_postcall_label;\\n28816:       /* ??? This view is at last_label, not last_label-1, but we\\n28817: \\t could only assume view at last_label-1 is zero if we could\\n28818: \\t assume calls always have length greater than one.  This is\\n28819: \\t probably true in general, though there might be a rare\\n28820: \\t exception to this rule, e.g. if a call insn is optimized out\\n28821: \\t by target magic.  Then, even the -1 in the label will be\\n28822: \\t wrong, which might invalidate the range.  Anyway, using view,\\n28823: \\t though technically possibly incorrect, will work as far as\\n28824: \\t ranges go: since L-1 is in the middle of the call insn,\\n28825: \\t (L-1).0 and (L-1).V shouldn't make any difference, and having\\n28826: \\t the loclist entry refer to the .loc entry might be useful, so\\n28827: \\t leave it like this.  */\\n28828:       newloc->view = view;\\n28829:     }\\n28830: \\n28831:   if (var_loc_p && flag_debug_asm)\\n28832:     {\\n28833:       const char *name, *sep, *patstr;\\n28834:       if (decl && DECL_NAME (decl))\\n28835: \\tname = IDENTIFIER_POINTER (DECL_NAME (decl));\\n28836:       else\\n28837: \\tname = \\\"\\\";\\n28838:       if (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_LOC (loc_note))\\n28839: \\t{\\n28840: \\t  sep = \\\" => \\\";\\n28841: \\t  patstr = str_pattern_slim (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_LOC (loc_note));\\n28842: \\t}\\n28843:       else\\n28844: \\t{\\n28845: \\t  sep = \\\" \\\";\\n28846: \\t  patstr = \\\"RESET\\\";\\n28847: \\t}\\n28848:       fprintf (asm_out_file, \\\"\\\\t%s DEBUG %s%s%s\\\\n\\\", ASM_COMMENT_START,\\n28849: \\t       name, sep, patstr);\\n28850:     }\\n28851: \\n28852:   last_var_location_insn = next_real;\\n28853:   last_in_cold_section_p = in_cold_section_p;\\n28854: }\\n28855: \\n28856: /* Check whether BLOCK, a lexical block, is nested within OUTER, or is\\n28857:    OUTER itself.  If BOTHWAYS, check not only that BLOCK can reach\\n28858:    OUTER through BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT links, but also that there is a\\n28859:    path from OUTER to BLOCK through BLOCK_SUBBLOCKs and\\n28860:    BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN links.  */\\n28861: static bool\\n28862: block_within_block_p (tree block, tree outer, bool bothways)\\n28863: {\\n28864:   if (block == outer)\\n28865:     return true;\\n28866: \\n28867:   /* Quickly check that OUTER is up BLOCK's supercontext chain.  */\\n28868:   for (tree context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (block);\\n28869:        context != outer;\\n28870:        context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (context))\\n28871:     if (!context || TREE_CODE (context) != BLOCK)\\n28872:       return false;\\n28873: \\n28874:   if (!bothways)\\n28875:     return true;\\n28876: \\n28877:   /* Now check that each block is actually referenced by its\\n28878:      parent.  */\\n28879:   for (tree context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (block); ;\\n28880:        context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (context))\\n28881:     {\\n28882:       if (BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN (context))\\n28883: \\t{\\n28884: \\t  gcc_assert (!BLOCK_SUBBLOCKS (context));\\n28885: \\t  context = BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN (context);\\n28886: \\t}\\n28887:       for (tree sub = BLOCK_SUBBLOCKS (context);\\n28888: \\t   sub != block;\\n28889: \\t   sub = BLOCK_CHAIN (sub))\\n28890: \\tif (!sub)\\n28891: \\t  return false;\\n28892:       if (context == outer)\\n28893: \\treturn true;\\n28894:       else\\n28895: \\tblock = context;\\n28896:     }\\n28897: }\\n28898: \\n28899: /* Called during final while assembling the marker of the entry point\\n28900:    for an inlined function.  */\\n28901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL28800-28900\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "28800: \tcall_arg_locations = ca_loc;\n28801:       call_arg_loc_last = ca_loc;\n28802:     }\n28803:   else if (loc_note != NULL_RTX && !NOTE_DURING_CALL_P (loc_note))\n28804:     {\n28805:       newloc->label = last_label;\n28806:       newloc->view = view;\n28807:     }\n28808:   else\n28809:     {\n28810:       if (!last_postcall_label)\n28811: \t{\n28812: \t  sprintf (loclabel, \"%s-1\", last_label);\n28813: \t  last_postcall_label = ggc_strdup (loclabel);\n28814: \t}\n28815:       newloc->label = last_postcall_label;\n28816:       /* ??? This view is at last_label, not last_label-1, but we\n28817: \t could only assume view at last_label-1 is zero if we could\n28818: \t assume calls always have length greater than one.  This is\n28819: \t probably true in general, though there might be a rare\n28820: \t exception to this rule, e.g. if a call insn is optimized out\n28821: \t by target magic.  Then, even the -1 in the label will be\n28822: \t wrong, which might invalidate the range.  Anyway, using view,\n28823: \t though technically possibly incorrect, will work as far as\n28824: \t ranges go: since L-1 is in the middle of the call insn,\n28825: \t (L-1).0 and (L-1).V shouldn't make any difference, and having\n28826: \t the loclist entry refer to the .loc entry might be useful, so\n28827: \t leave it like this.  */\n28828:       newloc->view = view;\n28829:     }\n28830: \n28831:   if (var_loc_p && flag_debug_asm)\n28832:     {\n28833:       const char *name, *sep, *patstr;\n28834:       if (decl && DECL_NAME (decl))\n28835: \tname = IDENTIFIER_POINTER (DECL_NAME (decl));\n28836:       else\n28837: \tname = \"\";\n28838:       if (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_LOC (loc_note))\n28839: \t{\n28840: \t  sep = \" => \";\n28841: \t  patstr = str_pattern_slim (NOTE_VAR_LOCATION_LOC (loc_note));\n28842: \t}\n28843:       else\n28844: \t{\n28845: \t  sep = \" \";\n28846: \t  patstr = \"RESET\";\n28847: \t}\n28848:       fprintf (asm_out_file, \"\\t%s DEBUG %s%s%s\\n\", ASM_COMMENT_START,\n28849: \t       name, sep, patstr);\n28850:     }\n28851: \n28852:   last_var_location_insn = next_real;\n28853:   last_in_cold_section_p = in_cold_section_p;\n28854: }\n28855: \n28856: /* Check whether BLOCK, a lexical block, is nested within OUTER, or is\n28857:    OUTER itself.  If BOTHWAYS, check not only that BLOCK can reach\n28858:    OUTER through BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT links, but also that there is a\n28859:    path from OUTER to BLOCK through BLOCK_SUBBLOCKs and\n28860:    BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN links.  */\n28861: static bool\n28862: block_within_block_p (tree block, tree outer, bool bothways)\n28863: {\n28864:   if (block == outer)\n28865:     return true;\n28866: \n28867:   /* Quickly check that OUTER is up BLOCK's supercontext chain.  */\n28868:   for (tree context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (block);\n28869:        context != outer;\n28870:        context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (context))\n28871:     if (!context || TREE_CODE (context) != BLOCK)\n28872:       return false;\n28873: \n28874:   if (!bothways)\n28875:     return true;\n28876: \n28877:   /* Now check that each block is actually referenced by its\n28878:      parent.  */\n28879:   for (tree context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (block); ;\n28880:        context = BLOCK_SUPERCONTEXT (context))\n28881:     {\n28882:       if (BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN (context))\n28883: \t{\n28884: \t  gcc_assert (!BLOCK_SUBBLOCKS (context));\n28885: \t  context = BLOCK_FRAGMENT_ORIGIN (context);\n28886: \t}\n28887:       for (tree sub = BLOCK_SUBBLOCKS (context);\n28888: \t   sub != block;\n28889: \t   sub = BLOCK_CHAIN (sub))\n28890: \tif (!sub)\n28891: \t  return false;\n28892:       if (context == outer)\n28893: \treturn true;\n28894:       else\n28895: \tblock = context;\n28896:     }\n28897: }\n28898: \n28899: /* Called during final while assembling the marker of the entry point\n28900:    for an inlined function.  */\n28901: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL28800-28900"

  "content": "{\"content\":\"28900:    for an inlined function.  */\\n28901: \\n28902: static void\\n28903: dwarf2out_inline_entry (tree block)\\n28904: {\\n28905:   gcc_assert (debug_inline_points);\\n28906: \\n28907:   /* If we can't represent it, don't bother.  */\\n28908:   if (!(dwarf_version >= 3 || !dwarf_strict))\\n28909:     return;\\n28910: \\n28911:   gcc_assert (DECL_P (block_ultimate_origin (block)));\\n28912: \\n28913:   /* Sanity check the block tree.  This would catch a case in which\\n28914:      BLOCK got removed from the tree reachable from the outermost\\n28915:      lexical block, but got retained in markers.  It would still link\\n28916:      back to its parents, but some ancestor would be missing a link\\n28917:      down the path to the sub BLOCK. 98. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 29550,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"29400: dwarf2out_set_ignored_loc (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\\n29401: \\t\\t\\t   const char *filename)\\n29402: {\\n29403:   dw_fde_ref fde = cfun->fde;\\n29404: \\n29405:   fde->ignored_debug = false;\\n29406:   set_cur_line_info_table (current_function_section ());\\n29407: \\n29408:   dwarf2out_source_line (line, column, filename, 0, true);\\n29409: }\\n29410: \\n29411: /* Record the beginning of a new source file.  */\\n29412: \\n29413: static void\\n29414: dwarf2out_start_source_file (unsigned int lineno, const char *filename)\\n29415: {\\n29416: #ifdef CODEVIEW_DEBUGGING_INFO\\n29417:   if (codeview_debuginfo_p ())\\n29418:     codeview_start_source_file (filename);\\n29419: #endif\\n29420: \\n29421:   if (debug_info_level >= DINFO_LEVEL_VERBOSE)\\n29422:     {\\n29423:       macinfo_entry e;\\n29424:       e.code = DW_MACINFO_start_file;\\n29425:       e.lineno = lineno;\\n29426:       e.info = ggc_strdup (filename);\\n29427:       vec_safe_push (macinfo_table, e);\\n29428:     }\\n29429: }\\n29430: \\n29431: /* Record the end of a source file.  */\\n29432: \\n29433: static void\\n29434: dwarf2out_end_source_file (unsigned int lineno ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED)\\n29435: {\\n29436:   if (debug_info_level >= DINFO_LEVEL_VERBOSE)\\n29437:     {\\n29438:       macinfo_entry e;\\n29439:       e.code = DW_MACINFO_end_file;\\n29440:       e.lineno = lineno;\\n29441:       e.info = NULL;\\n29442:       vec_safe_push (macinfo_table, e);\\n29443:     }\\n29444: }\\n29445: \\n29446: /* Called from debug_define in toplev.cc.     "content": "29400: dwarf2out_set_ignored_loc (unsigned int line, unsigned int column,\n29401: \t\t\t   const char *filename)\n29402: {\n29403:   dw_fde_ref fde = cfun->fde;\n29404: \n29405:   fde->ignored_debug = false;\n29406:   set_cur_line_info_table (current_function_section ());\n29407: \n29408:   dwarf2out_source_line (line, column, filename, 0, true);\n29409: }\n29410: \n29411: /* Record the beginning of a new source file.  */\n29412: \n29413: static void\n29414: dwarf2out_start_source_file (unsigned int lineno, const char *filename)\n29415: {\n29416: #ifdef CODEVIEW_DEBUGGING_INFO\n29417:   if (codeview_debuginfo_p ())\n29418:     codeview_start_source_file (filename);\n29419: #endif\n29420: \n29421:   if (debug_info_level >= DINFO_LEVEL_VERBOSE)\n29422:     {\n29423:       macinfo_entry e;\n29424:       e.code = DW_MACINFO_start_file;\n29425:       e.lineno = lineno;\n29426:       e.info = ggc_strdup (filename);\n29427:       vec_safe_push (macinfo_table, e);\n29428:     }\n29429: }\n29430: \n29431: /* Record the end of a source file.  */\n29432: \n29433: static void\n29434: dwarf2out_end_source_file (unsigned int lineno ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED)\n29435: {\n29436:   if (debug_info_level >= DINFO_LEVEL_VERBOSE)\n29437:     {\n29438:       macinfo_entry e;\n29439:       e.code = DW_MACINFO_end_file;\n29440:       e.lineno = lineno;\n29441:       e.info = NULL;\n29442:       vec_safe_push (macinfo_table, e);\n29443:     }\n29444: }\n29445: \n29446: /* Called from debug_define in toplev.cc. */\n29476: \n29477: static void\n29478: dwarf2out_undef (unsigned int lineno ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED,\n29479: \t\t const char *buffer ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED)\n29480: {\n29481:   if (debug_info_level >= DINFO_LEVEL_VERBOSE)\n29482:     {\n29483:       macinfo_entry e;\n29484:       /* Insert a dummy first entry to be able to optimize the whole\n29485: \t predefined macro block using DW_MACRO_import. \\\"wm4.\\\" : \\\"wm8.\\\", 4);\\n29714:   tail = grp_name + 4;\\n29715:   if (encoded_filename_len)\\n29716:     {\\n29717:       for (i = 0; base[i]; i++)\\n29718: \\tif (ISIDNUM (base[i]) || base[i] == '.')\\n29719: \\t  *tail++ = base[i];\\n29720:       *tail++ = '.';\\n29721:     }\\n29722:   memcpy (tail, linebuf, linebuf_len);\\n29723:   tail += linebuf_len;\\n29724:   *tail++ = '.';\\n29725:   for (i = 0; i < 16; i++)\\n29726:     sprintf (tail + i * 2, \\\"%02x\\\", checksum[i] & 0xff);\\n29727: \\n29728:   /* Construct a macinfo_entry for DW_MACRO_import\\n29729:      in the empty vector entry before the first define/undef.  */\\n29730:   inc = &(*macinfo_table)[idx - 1];\\n29731:   inc->code = DW_MACRO_import;\\n29732:   inc->lineno = 0;\\n29733:   inc->info = ggc_strdup (grp_name);\\n29734:   if (!*macinfo_htab)\\n29735:     *macinfo_htab = new macinfo_hash_type (10);\\n29736:   /* Avoid emitting duplicates.  */\\n29737:   slot = (*macinfo_htab)->find_slot (inc, INSERT);\\n29738:   if (*slot != NULL)\\n29739:     {\\n29740:       inc->code = 0;\\n29741:       inc->info = NULL;\\n29742:       /* If such an entry has been used before, just emit\\n29743: \\t a DW_MACRO_import op.  */\\n29744:       inc = *slot;\\n29745:       output_macinfo_op (inc);\\n29746:       /* And clear all macinfo_entry in the range to avoid emitting them\\n29747: \\t in the second pass.  */\\n29748:       for (i = idx; macinfo_table->iterate (i, &cur) && i < idx + count; i++)\\n29749: \\t{\\n29750: \\t  cur->code = 0;\\n29751: \\t  cur->info = NULL;\\n29752: \\t}\\n29753:     }\\n29754:   else\\n29755:     {\\n29756:       *slot = inc;\\n29757:       inc->lineno = (*macinfo_htab)->elements ();\\n29758:       output_macinfo_op (inc);\\n29759:     }\\n29760:   return count;\\n29761: }\\n29762: \\n29763: /* Save any strings needed by the macinfo table in the debug str\\n29764:    table.  All strings must be collected into the table by the time\\n29765:    index_string is called.  */\\n29766: \\n29767: static void\\n29768: save_macinfo_strings (void)\\n29769: {\\n29770:   unsigned       {\n29778:           /* Match the logic in output_macinfo_op to decide on\n29779:              indirect strings.  */\n29780:           case DW_MACINFO_define:\n29781:           case DW_MACINFO_undef:\n29782:             len = strlen (ref->info) + 1;\n29783: \t    if ((!dwarf_strict || dwarf_version >= 5)\n29784:                 && len > (unsigned) dwarf_offset_size\n29785:                 && !DWARF2_INDIRECT_STRING_SUPPORT_MISSING_ON_TARGET\n29786:                 && (debug_str_section->common.flags & SECTION_MERGE) != 0)\n29787:               set_indirect_string (find_AT_string (ref->info));\n29788:             break;\n29789: \t  case DW_MACINFO_start_file:\n29790: \t    /* -gsplit-dwarf -g3 will also output filename as indirect\n29791: \t       string.  */\n29792: \t    if (!dwarf_split_debug_info)\n29793: \t      break;\n29794: \t    /* Fall through. */\n29795: \t  case DW_MACRO_define_strp:\n29796: \t  case DW_MACRO_undef_strp:\n29797: \t  case DW_MACRO_define_strx:\n29798: \t  case DW_MACRO_undef_strx:\n29799:             set_indirect_string (find_AT_string (ref->info));\n29800:             break;\n29801:           default:\n29802:             break;\n29803:         }\n29804:     }\n29805: }\n29806: \n29807: /* Output macinfo section(s).  */\n29808: \n29809: static void\n29810: output_macinfo (const char *debug_line_label, bool early_lto_debug)\n29811: {\n29812:   unsigned i;\n29813:   unsigned long length = vec_safe_length (macinfo_table);\n29814:   macinfo_entry *ref;\n29815:   vec<macinfo_entry, va_gc> *files = NULL;\n29816:   macinfo_hash_type *macinfo_htab = NULL;\n29817:   char dl_section_ref[MAX_ARTIFICIAL_LABEL_BYTES];\n29818: \n29819:   if (! length)\n29820:     return;\n29821: \n29822:   /* output_macinfo* uses these interchangeably.  */\n29823:   gcc_assert ((int) DW_MACINFO_define == (int) DW_MACRO_define\n29824: \t      && (int) DW_MACINFO_undef == (int) DW_MACRO_undef\n29825: \t      && (int) DW_MACINFO_start_file == (int) DW_MACRO_start_file\n29826: \t      && (int) DW_MACINFO_end_file == (int) DW_MACRO_end_file);\n29827: \n29828:   /* AIX Assembler inserts the length, so adjust the reference to match the\n29829:      offset expected by debuggers.  */\n29830:   strcpy (dl_section_ref, debug_line_label);\n29831:   if (XCOFF_DEBUGGING_INFO)\n29832:     strcat (dl_section_ref, DWARF_INITIAL_LENGTH_SIZE_STR);\n29833: \n29834:   /* For .debug_macro emit the section header.  */\n29835:   if (!dwarf_strict || dwarf_version >= 5)\n29836:     {\n29837:       dw2_asm_output_data (2, dwarf_version >= 5 ? 5 : 4,\n29838: \t\t\t   \"DWARF macro version number\");\n29839:       if (dwarf_offset_size == 8)\n29840: \tdw2_asm_output_data (1, 3, \"Flags: 64-bit, lineptr present\");\n29841:       else\n29842: \tdw2_asm_output_data (1, 2, \"Flags: 32-bit, lineptr present\");\n29843:       dw2_asm_output_offset (dwarf_offset_size, debug_line_label,\n29844:                              debug_line_section, NULL);\n29845:     }\n29846: \n29847:   /* In the first loop, it emits the primary .debug_macinfo section\n29848:      and after each emitted op the macinfo_entry is cleared.\n29849:      If a longer range of define/undef ops can be optimized using\n29850:      DW_MACRO_import, the DW_MACRO_import op is emitted and kept in\n29851: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL29700-29850"
  }
}

\t      loc->dw_loc_opc = dwarf_OP (DW_OP_implicit_pointer);\n31406: \t      loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_class = dw_val_class_die_ref;\n31407: \t      loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry = NULL;\n31408: \t loc->dw_loc_oprnd2.v.val_int = offset;\n31412: \t      return true;\n31413: \t    }\n31414: \t}\n31415:     }\n31416:   return false;\n31417: }\n31418: \n31419: /* Helper function for resolve_addr, handle one location\n31420:    expression, return false if at least one CONST_STRING or SYMBOL_REF in\n31421:    the location list couldn't be resolved.  */\n31422: \n31423: static bool\n31424: resolve_addr_in_expr (dw_attr_node *a, dw_loc_descr_ref loc)\n31425: {\n31426:   dw_loc_descr_ref keep = NULL;\n31427:   for (dw_loc_descr_ref prev = NULL; loc; prev = loc, loc = loc->dw_loc_next)\n31428:     switch (loc->dw_loc_opc)\n31429:       {\n31430:       case DW_OP_addr:\n31431: \tif (!resolve_one_addr (&loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr))\n31432: \t  {\n31433: \t    if ((prev == NULL\n31434: \t\t || prev->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_piece\n31435: \t\t || prev->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_bit_piece)\n31436: \t\t&& loc->dw_loc_next\n31437: \t\t&& loc->dw_loc_next->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_stack_value\n31438: \t\t&& (!dwarf_strict || dwarf_version >= 5)\n31439: \t\t&& optimize_one_addr_into_implicit_ptr (loc))\n31440: \t      break;\n31441: \t    return false;\n31442: \t  }\n31443: \tbreak;\n31444:       case DW_OP_GNU_addr_index:\n31445:       case DW_OP_addrx:\n31446:       case DW_OP_GNU_const_index:\n31447:       case DW_OP_constx:\n31448: \tif ((loc->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_GNU_addr_index\n31449: \t     || loc->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_addrx)\n31450: \t    || ((loc->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_GNU_const_index\n31451: \t\t || loc->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_constx)\n31452: \t\t&& loc->dw_loc_dtprel))\n31453:           {\n31454:             rtx rtl = loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry->addr.rtl;\n31455:             if (!resolve_one_addr (&rtl))\n31456:               return false;\n31457:             remove_addr_table_entry (loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry);\n31458: \t    loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_entry\n31459: \t      = add_addr_table_entry (rtl, loc->dw_loc_dtprel\n31460: \t\t\t\t      ? ate_kind_rtx_dtprel : ate_kind_rtx);\n31461:           }\n31462: \tbreak;\n31463:       case DW_OP_const4u:\n31464:       case DW_OP_const8u:\n31465: \tif (loc->dw_loc_dtprel\n31466: \t    && !resolve_one_addr (&loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr))\n31467: \t  return false;\n31468: \tbreak;\n31469:       case DW_OP_plus_uconst:\n31470: \tif (size_of_loc_descr (loc)\n31471: \t    > size_of_int_loc_descriptor (loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_unsigned)\n31472: \t      + 1\n31473: \t    && loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_unsigned > 0)\n31474: \t  {\n31475: \t    dw_loc_descr_ref repl\n31476: \t      = int_loc_descriptor (loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_unsigned);\n31477: \t    add_loc_descr (&repl, new_loc_descr (DW_OP_plus, 0, 0));\n31478: \t    add_loc_descr (&repl, loc->dw_loc_next);\n31479: \t (&loc->dw_loc_oprnd2.v.val_addr))\n31485: \t  return false;\n31486: \tbreak;\n31487:       case DW_OP_implicit_pointer:\n31488:       case DW_OP_GNU_implicit_pointer:\n31489:       case DW_OP_GNU_parameter_ref:\n31490:       case DW_OP_GNU_variable_value:\n31491: \tif (loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_class == dw_val_class_decl_ref)\n31492: \t  {\n31493: \t    dw_die_ref ref\n31494: \t      = lookup_decl_die (loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_decl_ref);\n31495: \t    if (ref == NULL)\n31496: \t      return false;\n31497: \t    loc->dw_loc_oprnd1.val_class = dw_val_class_die_ref;\n31498: \t DW_AT_byte_size:\n31512: \t\tcase DW_AT_bit_size:\n31513: \t\tcase DW_AT_lower_bound:\n31514: \t\tcase DW_AT_upper_bound:\n31515: \t\tcase DW_AT_bit_stride:\n31516: \t\tcase DW_AT_count:\n31517: See if we have\\n31911: \\t   already recorded the result from a previous pass.  */\\n31912: \\tif (loc->replaced)\\n31913: \\t  *curr = loc->dw_loc_next;\\n31914: \\telse if (!loc->resolved_addr)\\n31915: \\t  {\\n31916: \\t    /* As things stand, we do not expect or allow one die to\\n31917: \\t       reference a suffix of another die's location list chain.\\n31918: \\t       References must be identical or completely separate.\\n31919: \\t       There is therefore no need to cache the result of this\\n31920: \\t       pass on any list other than the first; doing so\\n31921: \\t       would lead to unnecessary writes.  */\\n31922: \\t    while (*curr)\\n31923: \\t      {\\n31924: \\t\\tgcc_assert (!(*curr)->replaced && !(*curr)->resolved_addr);\\n31925: \\t\\tif (!resolve_addr_in_expr (a, (*curr)->expr))\\n31926: \\t\\t  {\\n31927: \\t\\t    dw_loc_list_ref next = (*curr)->dw_loc_next;\\n31928:                     dw_loc_descr_ref l = (*curr)->expr;\\n31929: \\n31930: \\t\\t    if (next && (*curr)->ll_symbol)\\n31931: \\t\\t      {\\n31932: \\t\\t\\tgcc_assert (!next->ll_symbol);\\n31933: \\t\\t\\tnext->ll_symbol = (*curr)->ll_symbol;\\n31934: \\t\\t\\tnext->vl_symbol = (*curr)->vl_symbol;\\n31935: \\t\\t      }\\n31936:                     if (dwarf_split_debug_info)\\n31937: \\t\\tloc->replaced = 1;\\n31951: \\t\\tloc->dw_loc_next = *start;\\n31952: \\t      }\\n31953: \\t  }\\n31954: \\tif (!*start)\\n31955: \\t  {\\n31956: \\t    remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\\n31957: \\t    ix--;\\n31958: \\t  }\\n31959: \\tbreak;\\n31960:       case dw_val_class_view_list:\\n31961: \\t{\\n31962: \\t  gcc_checking_assert (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_GNU_locviews);\\n31963: \\t  gcc_checking_assert (dwarf2out_locviews_in_attribute ());\\n31964: \\t  dw_val_node *llnode\\n31965: \\t    = view_list_to_loc_list_val_node (&a->dw_attr_val);\\n31966: \\t  /* If we no longer have a loclist, or it no longer needs\\n31967: \\t     views, drop this attribute.  */\\n31968: \\t  if (!llnode || !llnode->v.val_loc_list->vl_symbol)\\n31969: \\t    {\\n31970: \\t      remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\\n31971: \\t      ix--;\\n31972: \\t    }\\n31973: \\t  break;\\n31974: \\t}\\n31975:       case dw_val_class_loc:\\n31976: \\t{\\n31977: \\t  dw_loc_descr_ref l = AT_loc (a);\\n31978: \\t  /* DW_OP_GNU_variable_value DW_OP_stack_value or\\n31979: \\t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value in DW_AT_string_length can be converted\\n31980: \\t     into DW_OP_call4 or DW_OP_call4 DW_OP_deref, which is standard\\n31981: \\t     DWARF4 unlike DW_OP_GNU_variable_value.  Or for DWARF5\\n31982: \\t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value DW_OP_stack_value can be replaced\\n31983: \\t     with DW_FORM_ref referencing the same DIE as\\n31984: \\t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value used to reference.  */\\n31985: \\t  if (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_string_length\\n31986: \\t      && l\\n31987: \\t      && l->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_GNU_variable_value\\n31988: \\t      && (l->dw_loc_next == NULL\\n31989: \\t\\t  || (l->dw_loc_next->dw_loc_next == NULL\\n31990: \\t\\t      && l->dw_loc_next->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_stack_value)))\\n31991: \\t    {\\n31992: \\t      switch (optimize_string_length (a))\\n31993: \\t\\t{\\n31994: \\t\\tcase -1:\\n31995: \\t\\t  remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\\n31996: \\t\\t  ix--;\\n31997: \\t\\t  /* If we drop DW_AT_string_length, we need to drop also\\n31998: \\t\\t     DW_AT_{string_length_,}byte_size.  */\\n31999: \\t\\t  remove_AT_byte_size = true;\\n32000: \\t\\t  continue;\\n32001: \\t\\tdefault:\\n32002: \\t\\t  break;\\n32003: \\t\\tcase 1:\\n32004: \\t\\t  /* Even if we keep the optimized DW_AT_string_length,\\n32005: \\t\\t     it might have changed AT_class, so process it again.  */\\n32006: \\t\\t  ix--;\\n32007: \\t\\t  continue;\\n32008: \\t\\t}\\n32009: \\t    }\\n32010: \\t  /* For -gdwarf-2 don't attempt to optimize\\n32011: \\t     DW_AT_data_member_location containing\\n32012: \\t     DW_OP_plus_uconst - older consumers might\\n32013: \\t     rely on it being that op instead of a more complex,\\n32014: \\t     but shorter, location description.  */\\n32015: \\t  if ((dwarf_version > 2\\n32016: \\t       || a->dw_attr != DW_AT_data_member_location\\n32017: \\t       || l == NULL\\n32018: \\t       || l->dw_loc_opc != DW_OP_plus_uconst\\n32019: \\t       || l->dw_loc_next != NULL)\\n32020: \\t      && !resolve_addr_in_expr (a, l))\\n32021: \\t    {\\n32022: \\t      if (dwarf_split_debug_info)\\n32023: \\t\\tremove_loc_list_addr_table_entries (l);\\n32024: \\t      if (l != NULL\\n32025: \\t\\t  && l->dw_loc_next == NULL\\n32026: \\t\\t  && l->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_addr\\n32027: \\t\\t  && GET_CODE (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr) == SYMBOL_REF\\n32028: \\t\\t  && SYMBOL_REF_DECL (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr)\\n32029: \\t\\t  && a->dw_attr == DW_AT_location)\\n32030: \\t\\t{\\n32031: \\t\\t  tree decl = SYMBOL_REF_DECL (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr);\\n32032: \\t\\t  remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\\n32033: \\t\\t  ix--;\\n32034: \\t\\t  optimize_location_into_implicit_ptr (die, decl);\\n32035: \\t\\t  break;\\n32036: \\t\\t}\\n32037: \\t      if (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_string_length)\\n32038: \\t\\t/* If we drop DW_AT_string_length, we need to drop also\\n32039: \\t\\t   DW_AT_{string_length_,}byte_size.  */\\n32040: \\t\\tremove_AT_byte_size = true;\\n32041: \\t      remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\\n32042: \\t      ix--;\\n32043: \\t    }\\n32044: \\t  else\\n32045: \\t    mark_base_types (l);\\n32046: \\t}\\n32047: \\tbreak;\\n32048:       case dw_val_class_addr:\\n32049: \\tif (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_const_value\\n32050: \\t    && !resolve_one_addr (&a->dw_attr_val.v.val_addr))\\n32051: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL31900-32050\"}",

loclist, or it no longer needs\n31967: \t     views, drop this attribute.  */\n31968: \t  if (!llnode || !llnode->v.val_loc_list->vl_symbol)\n31969: \t    {\n31970: \t      remove_AT (die, DW_OP_GNU_variable_value DW_OP_stack_value or\n31979: \t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value in DW_AT_string_length can be converted\n31980: \t     into DW_OP_call4 or DW_OP_call4 DW_OP_deref, which is standard\n31981: \t     DWARF4 unlike DW_OP_GNU_variable_value.  Or for DWARF5\n31982: \t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value DW_OP_stack_value can be replaced\n31983: \t     with DW_FORM_ref referencing the same DIE as\n31984: \t     DW_OP_GNU_variable_value used to reference.  */\n31985: \t  if (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_string_length\n31986: \t      && l\n31987: \t      && l->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_GNU_variable_value\n31988: \t      && (l->dw_loc_next == NULL\n31989: \t\t  || (l->dw_loc_next->dw_loc_next == NULL\n31990: \t\t      && l->dw_loc_next->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_stack_value)))\n31991: \t    {\n31992: \t      switch (optimize_string_length (a))\n31993: \t\t{\n31994: \t\tcase -1:\n31995: \t\t  remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\n31996: \t\t  ix--;\n31997: \t\t  /* If we drop DW_AT_string_length, we need to drop also\n31998: \t\t     DW_AT_{string_length_,}byte_size.  */\n31999: \t\t  remove_AT_byte_size = true;\n32000: \t\t  continue;\n32001: \t\tdefault:\n32002: \t\t  break;\n32003: \t\tcase 1:\n32004: \t\t  /* Even if we keep the optimized DW_AT_string_length,\n32005: \t\t     it might have changed AT_class, so process it again.  */\n32006: \t\t  ix--;\n32007: \t\t  continue;\n32008: \t\t}\n32009: \t    }\n32010: \t  /* For -gdwarf-2 don't attempt to optimize\n32011: \t     DW_AT_data_member_location containing\n32012: \t     DW_OP_plus_uconst - older consumers might\n32013: \t     rely on it being that op instead of a more complex,\n32014: \t     but shorter, location description.  */\n32015: \t  if ((dwarf_version > 2\n32016: \t       || a->dw_attr != DW_AT_data_member_location\n32017: \t       || l == NULL\n32018: \t       || l->dw_loc_opc != DW_OP_plus_uconst\n32019: \t       || l->dw_loc_next != NULL)\n32020: \t      && !resolve_addr_in_expr (a, l))\n32021: \t    {\n32022: \t      if (dwarf_split_debug_info)\n32023: \t\tremove_loc_list_addr_table_entries (l);\n32024: \t      if (l != NULL\n32025: \t\t  && l->dw_loc_next == NULL\n32026: \t\t  && l->dw_loc_opc == DW_OP_addr\n32027: \t\t  && GET_CODE (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr) == SYMBOL_REF\n32028: \t\t  && SYMBOL_REF_DECL (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr)\n32029: \t\t  && a->dw_attr == DW_AT_location)\n32030: \t\t{\n32031: \t\t  tree decl = SYMBOL_REF_DECL (l->dw_loc_oprnd1.v.val_addr);\n32032: \t\t  remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\n32033: \t\t  ix--;\n32034: \t\t  optimize_location_into_implicit_ptr (die, decl);\n32035: \t\t  break;\n32036: \t\t}\n32037: \t      if (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_string_length)\n32038: \t\t/* If we drop DW_AT_string_length, we need to drop also\n32039: \t\t   DW_AT_{string_length_,}byte_size.  */\n32040: \t\tremove_AT_byte_size = true;\n32041: \t      remove_AT (die, a->dw_attr);\n32042: \t      ix--;\n32043: \t    }\n32044: \t  else\n32045: \t    mark_base_types (l);\n32046: \t}\n32047: \tbreak;\n32048:       case dw_val_class_addr:\n32049: \tif (a->dw_attr == DW_AT_const_value\n32050: \t    && !resolve_one_addr (&a->dw_attr_val.v.val_addr))\n32051: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL31900-32050"
  }
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"33650:   for (limbo_die_node *node = deferred_asm_name; node; node = node->next)\\n33651:     {\\n33652:       tree decl = node->created_for;\\n33653:       if (DECL_ASSEMBLER_NAME (decl) != DECL_NAME (decl)\\n33654: \\t  /* A missing DECL_ASSEMBLER_NAME can be a constant DIE that\\n33655: \\t     ended up in deferred_asm_name before we knew it was\\n33656: \\t     constant and never written to disk.  */\\n33657: \\t  && DECL_ASSEMBLER_NAME (decl))\\n33658: \\t{\\n33659: \\t  add_linkage_attr (node->die, decl);\\n33660: \\t  move_linkage_attr (node->die);\\n33661: \\t}\\n33662:     }\\n33663:   deferred_asm_name = NULL;\\n33664: \\n33665:   if (flag_eliminate_unused_debug_types)\\n33666:     prune_unused_types ();\\n33667: \\n33668:   /* Generate separate COMDAT sections for type DIEs. */\\n33669:   if (use_debug_types)\\n33670:     {\\n33671:       break_out_comdat_types (comp_unit_die ());\\n33672: \\n33673:       /* Each new type_unit DIE was added to the limbo die list when created.\\n33674:          Since these have all been added to comdat_type_list, clear the\\n33675:          limbo die list.  */\\n33676:       limbo_die_list = NULL;\\n33677: \\n33678:       /* For each new comdat type unit, copy declarations for incomplete\\n33679:          types to make the new unit self-contained (i.e., no direct\\n33680:          references to the main compile unit).  */\\n33681:       for (ctnode = comdat_type_list; ctnode != NULL; ctnode = ctnode->next)\\n33682:         copy_decls_for_unworthy_types (ctnode->root_die);\\n33683:       copy_decls_for_unworthy_types (comp_unit_die ());\\n33684: \\n33685:       /* In the process of copying declarations from one unit to another,\\n33686:          we may have left some declarations behind that are no longer\\n33687:          referenced.  Prune them.  */\\n33688:       prune_unused_types ();\\n33689:     }\\n33690: \\n33691:   /* Traverse the DIE's and note DIEs with DW_OP_GNU_variable_value still\\n33692:      with dw_val_class_decl_ref operand.  */\\n33693:   note_variable_value (comp_unit_die ());\\n33694:   for (limbo_die_node *node = cu_die_list; node; node = node->next)\\n33695:     note_variable_value (node->die);\\n33696:   for (ctnode = comdat_type_list; ctnode != NULL; ctnode = ctnode->next)\\n33697:     note_variable_value (ctnode->root_die);\\n33698:   for (limbo_die_node *node = limbo_die_list; node; node = node->next)\\n33699:     note_variable_value (node->die);\\n33700: \\n33701:   /* The AT_pubnames attribute needs to go in all skeleton dies, including\\n33702:      both the main_cu and all skeleton TUs.  Making this call unconditional\\n33703:      would end up either adding a second copy of the AT_pubnames attribute, or\\n33704:      requiring a special case in add_top_level_skeleton_die_attrs.  */\\n33705:   if (!dwarf_split_debug_info)\\n33706:     add_AT_pubnames (comp_unit_die ());\\n33707: \\n33708:   /* The early debug phase is now finished.  */\\n33709:   early_dwarf_finished = true;\\n33710:   if (dump_file)\\n33711:     {\\n33712:       fprintf (dump_file, \\\"EARLY DWARF for %s\\\\n\\\", filename);\\n33713:       print_die (comp_unit_die (), dump_file);\\n33714:     }\\n33715: \\n33716:   /* Generate CTF/BTF debug info.  */\\n33717:   if ((ctf_debug_info_level > CTFINFO_LEVEL_NONE\\n33718:        || btf_debuginfo_p ()) && lang_GNU_C ())\\n33719:     {\\n33720:       ctf_debug_init ();\\n33721:       ctf_debug_do_cu (comp_unit_die ());\\n33722:       for (limbo_die_node *node = limbo_die_list; node; node = node->next)\\n33723: \\tctf_debug_do_cu (node->die);\\n33724: \\n33725:       ctf_debug_early_finish (filename);\\n33726:     }\\n33727: \\n33728: #ifdef CODEVIEW_DEBUGGING_INFO\\n33729:   if (codeview_debuginfo_p ())\\n33730:     codeview_debug_early_finish (comp_unit_die ());\\n33731: #endif\\n33732: \\n33733:   /* Do not generate DWARF assembler now when not producing LTO bytecode.  */\\n33734:   if ((!flag_generate_lto && !flag_generate_offload)\\n33735:       /* FIXME: Disable debug info generation for (PE-)COFF targets since the\\n33736: \\t copy_lto_debug_sections operation of the simple object support in\\n33737: \\t libiberty is not implemented for them yet.  */\\n33738:       || TARGET_PECOFF || TARGET_COFF)\\n33739:     return;\\n33740: \\n33741:   /* Now as we are going to output for LTO initialize sections and labels\\n33742:      to the LTO variants.  We don't need a random-seed postfix as other\\n33743:      LTO sections as linking the LTO debug sections into one in a partial\\n33744:      link is fine.  */\\n33745:   init_sections_and_labels (true);\\n33746: \\n33747:   /* The output below is modeled after dwarf2out_finish with all\\n33748:      location related output removed and some LTO specific changes.\\n33749:      Some refactoring might make both smaller and easier to match up.  */\\n33750: \\n33751:   base_types.truncate (0);\\n33752:   for (ctnode = comdat_type_list; ctnode != NULL; ctnode = ctnode->next)\\n33753:     mark_base_types (ctnode->root_die);\\n33754:   mark_base_types (comp_unit_die ());\\n33755:   move_marked_base_types ();\\n33756: \\n33757:   /* Traverse the DIE's and add sibling attributes to those DIE's\\n33758:      that have children.  */\\n33759:   add_sibling_attributes (comp_unit_die ());\\n33760:   for (limbo_die_node *node = limbo_die_list; Input
{
  "content": "{\n  \"files\": [\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.h\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/debug.h\"\n    },\n    {\n \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/debug.h\",\n      \"symbol\": \"gcc_debug_hooks\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/debug.h\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dwarf2_debug_hooks\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/toplev.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dwarf2_debug_hooks\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dwarf2out_init\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dwarf2out_early_global_decl\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"new_die\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"new_die_raw\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"add_child_die\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dwarf2out_finish\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"output_comp_unit\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"output_die\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"output_line_info\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2out.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"output_one_line_info_table\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2asm.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dw2_asm_output_data\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"# GCC DWARF Debug Information Generation Chain\\n\\n## Overview\\nThe GCC DWARF debug information generation is structured in a multi-phase architecture where the front-end communicates with the debug back-end through a hook interface (`gcc_debug_hooks`), which is implemented by the DWARF2 subsystem.\\n\\n## Key Architecture Components\\n\\n### 1. Debug Hook Interface (gcc/debug.h)\\nThe `struct gcc_debug_hooks` defined in `gcc/debug.h` is the primary interface between the compiler front-end and the DWARF debug information generator. It includes hooks for:\\n\\n- **Initialization/Finalization**: `init()`, `finish()`, `early_finish()`\\n- **Function/Variable Processing**: `early_global_decl()`, `late_global_decl()`, `function_decl()`\\n- **Type Information**: `type_decl()`\\n- **Lexical Blocks**: `begin_block()`, `end_block()`, `ignore_block()`\\n- **Source Location Tracking**: `source_line()`, `begin_prologue()`, `end_prologue()`, `var_location()`\\n- **Module/Declaration Imports**: `imported_module_or_decl()`\\n- **Preprocessing Information**: `define()`, `undef()`, `start_source_file()`, `end_source_file()`\\n\\nThe global instance `debug_hooks` is set at runtime (in `gcc/toplev.cc`) to point to `dwarf2_debug_hooks` based on debugging options.\\n\\n### 2. DWARF DIE Creation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nDEBUG Information Entries (DIEs) are created through a two-level mechanism:\\n\\n- **`new_die_raw(enum dwarf_tag tag)`**: Low-level DIE allocation that creates an uninitialized `struct die_struct` with the specified DWARF tag. Located in `gcc/dwarf2out.cc`.\\n\\n- **`new_die(enum dwarf_tag tag, dw_die_ref parent_die, tree t)`**: Higher-level DIE creation that:\\n  - Calls `new_die_raw()` to allocate the DIE\\n  - Adds the DIE as a child to its parent via `add_child_die()`\\n  - Handles \\\"limbo DIEs\\\" (parentless DIEs) using a special limbo die list\\n  - Associates the DIE with tree nodes via lookup tables\\n\\n- **`add_child_die(dw_die_ref parent_die, dw_die_ref child_die)`**: Adds a DIE as the last child of another DIE, maintaining the DIE tree structure.\\n\\nThe `dw_die_ref` typedef (defined in `gcc/dwarf2out.h`) is a pointer to `struct die_struct`, the internal representation of a DWARF Debug Info Entry.\\n\\n### 3. DWARF Output Generation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nOnce DIEs are created, the DWARF sections are generated during the `dwarf2out_finish()` function, which:\\n\\n- **`output_comp_unit(dw_die_ref die, unsigned int depth, dw_die_ref *checksum)`**: Outputs a complete compilation unit\\n  - Calls `output_die()` recursively to output all DIEs in the unit\\n  - Handles abbreviation table generation\\n  - Manages DWARF section output\\n\\n- **`output_die(dw_die_ref die)`**: Recursively outputs a DIE and all its attributes:\\n  - Emits the DIE tag and attributes\\n  - Calls itself recursively for child DIEs\\n  - Uses attribute output functions to serialize DIE data\\n\\n### 4. Line Number Table Emission (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nSource location information is tracked and output via:\\n\\n- **`new_line_info_table()`**: Creates a new `dw_line_info_table` structure for a section, initializing line number state machine parameters (file number, line number, statement marker, view, etc.)\\n\\n- **`set_cur_line_info_table(section *sec)`**: Sets the current line info table for a specific section (text_section, cold_text_section, or other sections)\\n\\n- **`output_line_info(bool prologue_only)`**: Main function for outputting the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Generates the line number program header (version, minimum instruction length, etc.)\\n  - Outputs the file names table\\n  - Outputs the line number program state machine instructions\\n\\n- **`output_one_line_info_table(dw_line_info_table *table)`**: Outputs a single line number table into the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Encodes source line number information using the line number program state machine\\n  - Handles address setting (LI_set_address)\\n  - Handles line number updates (LI_set_line)\\n  - Handles file number changes (LI_set_file)\\n  - Handles column information (LI_set_column)\\n  - Handles discriminator values (LI_set_discriminator)\\n\\n### 5. Runtime Hook Dispatch (gcc/toplev.cc)\\nThe debug hooks are initialized in `toplev.cc`:\\n- The global `debug_hooks` pointer is assigned to `&dwarf2_debug_hooks` when DWARF2 debugging is enabled\\n- The `dwarf2_debug_hooks` structure contains function pointers to all DWARF2-specific implementations\\n- Hook implementations include functions like `dwarf2out_init()`, `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()`, `dwarf2out_source_line()`, etc.\\n\\n## Execution Flow\\n\\n1. **Early Debug Phase**: When `dwarf2out_init()` is called, it initializes data structures for DIE creation and early compilation unit setup\\n\\n2. **Compilation Phase**: As the compiler processes declarations and definitions, front-end calls debug hooks:\\n   - `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()` for declarations\\n   - `dwarf2out_late_global_decl()` for variable/function definitions with full information\\n   - `dwarf2out_function_decl()` for function definitions\\n   - `dwarf2out_source_line()` for source location tracking\\n   - `dwarf2out_var_location()` for variable location information\\n\\n3. **DIE Creation**: Debug hooks create DIEs using `new_die()` and `new_die_raw()`, building a tree structure via `add_child_die()`\\n\\n4. **Finish Phase**: At end of compilation, `dwarf2out_finish()` orchestrates:\\n   - Walking all created DIEs\\n   - Outputting compilation units via `output_comp_unit()`\\n   - Outputting DIEs recursively via `output_die()`\\n   - Generating the line number table via `output_line_info()`\\n\\n## Supporting Infrastructure\\n\\n- **gcc/dwarf2asm.cc**: Low-level DWARF assembly output functions (`dw2_asm_output_data()`, etc.)\\n- **gcc/dwarf2cfi.cc**: Call Frame Information (CFI) generation for exception handling\\n- **gcc/dwarf2ctf.cc**: CTF (Compact Type Format) debug info generation from DWARF DIEs\\n- **gcc/ctfc.cc**: CTF container management and type definition tracking\\n- **gcc/final.cc**: Final assembly phase, calls debug hooks for prologue/epilogue tracking\\n\\nThis architecture allows GCC to generate complete DWARF2 debug information through a clean hook-based interface, enabling support for multiple debug formats while maintaining a consistent internal representation based on DWARF DIEs.\"\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/answer.json"
}
